records.name: alluxio.node.label
records.description: The label of node where this process resides

records.name: alluxio.cacheability.timestamp.error.margin
records.description: The error margin when comparing timestamps in cacheability requirements to account for out of sync clocks between Alluxio and the UFS

records.name: alluxio.conf.dir
records.description: The directory of Alluxio configuration files. This property is only for internal use. To change the location, set environment variable $ALLUXIO_CONF_DIR instead.

records.name: alluxio.conf.dynamic.update.enabled
records.description: Whether to support dynamic update property.

records.name: alluxio.conf.validation.enabled
records.description: Whether to validate the configuration properties when initializing Alluxio clients or server process.

records.name: alluxio.debug
records.description: Set to true to enable debug mode which has additional logging and info in the Web UI.

records.name: alluxio.third.party.dir
records.description: The directory containing Alluxio third party jars.

records.name: alluxio.exit.collect.info
records.description: If true, the process will dump metrics and jstack into the log folder. This only applies to Alluxio master and worker processes.

records.name: alluxio.grpc.reflection.enabled
records.description: If true, grpc reflection will be enabled on alluxio grpc servers, including masters, workers, job masters and job workers. This makes grpc tools such as grpcurl or grpcui can send grpc requests to the master server easier without knowing the protobufs. This is a debug option.

records.name: alluxio.home
records.description: Alluxio installation directory.

records.name: alluxio.leak.detector.level
records.description: Set this to one of {DISABLED, SIMPLE, ADVANCED, PARANOID} to track resource leaks in the Alluxio codebase. DISABLED does not track any leaks. SIMPLE only samples resources, and doesn't track recent accesses, having a low overhead. ADVANCED is like simple, but tracks recent object accesses and has higher overhead. PARANOID tracks all objects and has the highest overhead. It is recommended to only use this value during testing.

records.name: alluxio.leak.detector.exit.on.leak
records.description: If set to true, the JVM will exit as soon as a leak is detected. Use only in testing environments.

records.name: alluxio.logger.type
records.description: This controls which logger the process uses. This is only set by test code.

records.name: alluxio.logs.dir
records.description: The path to store logs files of Alluxio servers. This property is only for internal use. To change the location, set environment variable $ALLUXIO_LOGS_DIR instead.

records.name: alluxio.user.logs.dir
records.description: The path to store logs of Alluxio command lines. This property is only for internal use. To change the location, set environment variable $ALLUXIO_USER_LOGS_DIR instead.

records.name: alluxio.metrics.conf.file
records.description: The file path of the metrics system configuration file. By default it is `metrics.properties` in the `conf` directory.

records.name: alluxio.metrics.executor.task.warn.size
records.description: When instrumenting an executor with InstrumentedExecutorService, if the number of active tasks (queued or running) is greater than this value, a warning log will be printed at the interval given by %s

records.name: alluxio.metrics.executor.task.warn.frequency
records.description: When instrumenting an executor withInstrumentedExecutorService, if the number of active tasks (queued or running) is greater than %s value, a warning log will be printed at the given interval

records.name: alluxio.metrics.key.including.unique.id.enabled
records.description: Whether to include the unique id such as hostname in the metrcis key.

records.name: alluxio.metrics.deprecated.metrics.enabled
records.description: Whether enable old metrics.

records.name: alluxio.metrics.current.metrics.enabled
records.description: Whether enable current metrics.

records.name: alluxio.metrics.sink.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable metrics sink.

records.name: alluxio.metrics.sink.class
records.description: The class of the metrics sink.

records.name: alluxio.metrics.sink.deps
records.description: A comma separated list of jar files or directories containing jar files that are required by the metrics sink.

records.name: alluxio.metrics.per.directory.enabled
records.description: Whether add the mount points as label for critical metrics

records.name: alluxio.metrics.per.directory.depth
records.description: The depth of alluxio path to attach as label to critical metrics

records.name: alluxio.network.connection.auth.timeout
records.description: Maximum time to wait for a connection (gRPC channel) to attempt to receive an authentication response.

records.name: alluxio.network.connection.health.check.timeout
records.description: Allowed duration for checking health of client connections (gRPC channels) before being assigned to a client. If a connection does not become active within configured time, it will be shut down and a new connection will be created for the client

records.name: alluxio.network.connection.server.shutdown.timeout
records.description: Maximum time to wait for gRPC server to stop on shutdown

records.name: alluxio.network.connection.shutdown.graceful.timeout
records.description: Maximum time to wait for connections (gRPC channels) to stop on shutdown

records.name: alluxio.network.connection.shutdown.timeout
records.description: Maximum time to wait for connections (gRPC channels) to stop after graceful shutdown attempt.

records.name: alluxio.network.host.resolution.timeout
records.description: During startup of the Master and Worker processes Alluxio needs to ensure that they are listening on externally resolvable and reachable host names. To do this, Alluxio will automatically attempt to select an appropriate host name if one was not explicitly specified. This represents the maximum amount of time spent waiting to determine if a candidate host name is resolvable over the network.

records.name: alluxio.network.netty.heartbeat.timeout
records.description: The amount of time the server will wait before closing a netty connection if there has not been any incoming traffic. The client will periodically heartbeat when there is no activity on a connection. This value should be the same on the clients and server.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.shutdown.timeout
records.description: Maximum amount of time to wait until the netty server is shutdown (regardless of the quiet period).

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.backlog
records.description: Netty socket option for SO_BACKLOG: the number of connections queued.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.buffer.send
records.description: Netty socket option for SO_SNDBUF: the proposed buffer size that will be used for sends.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.buffer.receive
records.description: Netty socket option for SO_RCVBUF: the proposed buffer size that will be used for receives.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.data.ioworker.num
records.description: Number of RDMA IO worker threads for the Alluxio RDMA data node. This is only used when RDMA data transport is enabled.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.data.operation.timeout.ms
records.description: Maximum time to wait for an RDMA operation to complete before timing out. Covers the full round-trip including Worker-side UFS I/O (which may involve cold reads from S3 or remote NFS). Set higher than the expected worst-case UFS latency. This is only used when RDMA data transport is enabled.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.data.inline.threshold
records.description: This is only used when RDMA data transport is enabled for user clients. The threshold in bytes under which RDMA write will be attempted as inline.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.data.channel.idle.timeout.ms
records.description: Idle timeout for RDMA channels.If there is no read/write activity on the channel for this duration,the channel will be closed. This is only used when RDMA data transport is enabled.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.data.trace.enabled
records.description: Whether to start printing RDMA data transport trace stats report.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.data.trace.print.interval.ms
records.description: Interval for printing out RDMA data transport trace statistics logs.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.data.buffer.pool.initial.size
records.description: Initial size budget of the RDMA registered buffer pool at startup.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.data.buffer.pool.prewarm.size
records.description: Amount of RDMA registered buffer pool memory to pre-warm in the 4MB chunk bucket at startup. This must not exceed alluxio.network.rdma.data.buffer.pool.initial.size.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.data.buffer.pool.max.size
records.description: Maximum size of the RDMA registered buffer pool. The pool will not expand beyond this limit. Prefetching stops when the pool is full.

records.name: alluxio.site.conf.dir
records.description: Comma-separated search path for %s.

records.name: alluxio.site.conf.rocks.inode.file
records.description: Path of file containing RocksDB inode store configuration. A template configuration cab be found at ${%s}/rocks-inode.ini.template. See https://github.com/facebook/rocksdb/blob/main/examples/rocksdb_option_file_example.ini for more information on RocksDB configuration files. If unset then a default configuration will be used.

records.name: alluxio.site.conf.rocks.block.file
records.description: Path of file containing RocksDB block store configuration. A template configuration cab be found at ${%s}/rocks-block.ini.template. See https://github.com/facebook/rocksdb/blob/main/examples/rocksdb_option_file_example.ini for more information on RocksDB configuration files. If unset then a default configuration will be used.

records.name: alluxio.network.ip.address.used
records.description: If true, when alluxio.<service_name>.hostname and alluxio.<service_name>.bind.host of a service not specified, use IP as the connect host of the service.

records.name: alluxio.test.mode
records.description: Flag used only during tests to allow special behavior.

records.name: alluxio.tmp.dirs
records.description: The path(s) to store Alluxio temporary files, use commas as delimiters. If multiple paths are specified, one will be selected at random per temporary file. Currently, only files to be uploaded to object stores are stored in these paths.

records.name: alluxio.version
records.description: Version of Alluxio. User should never modify this property.

records.name: alluxio.web.file.info.enabled
records.description: Whether detailed file information are enabled for the web UI.

records.name: alluxio.web.resources
records.description: Path to the web UI resources. User should never modify this property.

records.name: alluxio.web.threads
records.description: How many threads to serve Alluxio web UI.

records.name: alluxio.web.cors.enabled
records.description: Set to true to enable Cross-Origin Resource Sharing for RESTful APIendpoints.

records.name: alluxio.web.cors.allow.credential
records.description: Enable request include credential.

records.name: alluxio.web.cors.allow.headers
records.description: Which headers is allowed for cors. use * allow all any header.

records.name: alluxio.web.cors.allow.methods
records.description: Which methods is allowed for cors. use * allow all any method.

records.name: alluxio.web.cors.allow.origins
records.description: Which origins is allowed for cors. use * allow all any origin.

records.name: alluxio.web.cors.exposed.headers
records.description: Which headers are allowed to set in response when access cross-origin resource. use * allow all any header.

records.name: alluxio.web.cors.max.age
records.description: Maximum number of seconds the results can be cached. -1 means no cache.

records.name: alluxio.web.refresh.interval
records.description: The amount of time to await before refreshing the Web UI if it is set to auto refresh.

records.name: alluxio.web.threaddump.log.enabled
records.description: Whether thread information is also printed to the log when the thread dump api is accessed

records.name: alluxio.web.ui.enabled
records.description: Whether the master/worker will have Web UI enabled. If set to false, the master/worker will not have Web UI page, but the RESTful endpoints and metrics will still be available.

records.name: alluxio.work.dir
records.description: The directory to use for Alluxio's working directory. By default, the journal, logs, and under file storage data (if using local filesystem) are written here.

records.name: alluxio.k8s.env.deployment
records.description: If Alluxio is deployed in K8s environment.

records.name: alluxio.dynamic.configuration.etcd.sync.interval
records.description: Interval for syncing the dynamic configurations from etcd

records.name: alluxio.dynamic.configuration.etcd.timeout
records.description: Timeout for accessing etcd cluster in dynamic configuration management

records.name: alluxio.underfs.xattr.change.enabled
records.description: When enabled, Alluxio allows user to set/get xAttr from/to UFS. There will be different implementation methods in different UFS, implemented like HdfsUFS.setXattr, ObjectUFS.setTagging, or other ways.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.strict.version.match.enabled
records.description: When enabled, Alluxio finds the UFS connector by strict version matching. Otherwise only version prefix is compared.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.allow.set.owner.failure
records.description: Whether to allow setting owner in UFS to fail. When set to true, it is possible file or directory owners diverge between Alluxio and UFS.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.checksum.type
records.description: UFS checksum type.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.cleanup.enabled
records.description: Whether or not to clean up under file storage periodically.Some ufs operations may not be completed and cleaned up successfully in normal ways and leave some intermediate data that needs periodical cleanup.If enabled, all the mount points will be cleaned up when a leader master starts or cleanup interval is reached. This should be used sparingly.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.listing.length
records.description: The maximum number of directory entries to list in a single query to under file system. If the total number of entries is greater than the specified length, multiple queries will be issued.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.logging.threshold
records.description: Logging a UFS API call when it takes more time than the threshold.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.gcs.default.mode
records.description: Mode (in octal notation) for GCS objects if mode cannot be discovered.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.gcs.directory.suffix
records.description: Directories are represented in GCS as zero-byte objects named with the specified suffix.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.gcs.retry.initial.delay
records.description: Initial delay before attempting the retry on the ufs

records.name: alluxio.underfs.gcs.retry.max.delay
records.description: Maximum delay before attempting the retry on the ufs

records.name: alluxio.underfs.gcs.retry.delay.multiplier
records.description: Delay multiplier while retrying requests on the ufs

records.name: alluxio.underfs.gcs.retry.jitter
records.description: Enable delay jitter while retrying requests on the ufs

records.name: alluxio.underfs.gcs.retry.total.duration
records.description: Maximum retry duration on the ufs

records.name: alluxio.underfs.gcs.retry.max
records.description: Maximum Number of retries on the ufs

records.name: alluxio.underfs.gcs.multipart.upload.enabled
records.description: If true, use multipart upload (compose) to write to GCS.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.gcs.multipart.upload.partition.size
records.description: Maximum size of each part when using GCS multipart upload.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.gcs.multipart.upload.threads
records.description: Number of threads for parallel multipart upload to GCS.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.gcs.multipart.upload.buffer.number
records.description: Number of byte array buffers pooled for GCS multipart upload. A value <= 0 means unlimited.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.hdfs.configuration
records.description: Location of the HDFS configuration file to overwrite the default HDFS client configuration. Note that, these files must be availableon every node.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.hdfs.impl
records.description: The implementation class of the HDFS as the under storage system.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.hdfs.prefixes
records.description: Optionally, specify which prefixes should run through the HDFS implementation of UnderFileSystem. The delimiter is any whitespace and/or ','.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.ozone.prefixes
records.description: Specify which prefixes should run through the Ozone implementation of UnderFileSystem. The delimiter is any whitespace and/or ','. The default value is "o3fs://,ofs://".

records.name: alluxio.underfs.hdfs.remote
records.description: Boolean indicating whether or not the under storage worker nodes are remote with respect to Alluxio worker nodes. If set to true, Alluxio will not attempt to discover locality information from the under storage because locality is impossible. This will improve performance. The default value is true.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.hdfs.har.enabled
records.description: Whether auto decompress hdfs har file.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.hdfs.har.fs.cache.max.size
records.description: The maximum number of cached har filesystems. Since creating each HAR filesystem incurs some additional overhead, we use caching here.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.hdfs.har.fs.cache.expired.time
records.description: The expired time of har filesystem cache.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.hdfs.trash.enabled
records.description: If enabled, when a user deletes a file that's stored in HDFS via Alluxio, Alluxio will make use of HDFS's trash and try to move it to the trash directory instead of deleting it directly from HDFS. Note that HDFS must be configured to enable the trash functionality (in 'core-site.xml'). If trashing is not enabled in HDFS, Alluxio will delete the file directly, regardless of this configuration.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.io.threads
records.description: Number of threads used for UFS IO operation

records.name: alluxio.underfs.local.skip.broken.symlinks
records.description: When set to true, any time the local underfs lists a broken symlink, it will treat the entry as if it didn't exist at all.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.persistence.async.temp.dir
records.description: The temporary directory used for async persistence in the ufs

records.name: alluxio.underfs.web.connnection.timeout
records.description: Default timeout for a http connection.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.object.store.streaming.upload.part.timeout
records.description: Timeout for uploading part when using streaming uploads.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.object.store.multipart.upload.async
records.description: Set this to true to upload part asynchronously when using multipart upload in UFS. OOM may occur when uploading large files.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.object.store.multipart.upload.timeout
records.description: Timeout for uploading part when using multipart upload.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.object.store.breadcrumbs.enabled
records.description: Set this to false to prevent Alluxio from creating zero byte objects during read or list operations on object store UFS. Leaving this on enables more efficient listing of prefixes.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.object.store.multi.range.chunk.size
records.description: Default chunk size for ranged reads from multi-range object input streams.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.object.store.service.threads
records.description: The number of threads in executor pool for parallel object store UFS operations, such as directory renames and deletes.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.object.store.skip.parent.directory.creation
records.description: Do not create parent directory for new files. Object stores generally uses prefix which is not required for creating new files. Skipping parent directory is recommended for better performance. Set this to false if the object store requires prefix creation for new files.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.object.store.mount.shared.publicly
records.description: Whether or not to share object storage under storage system mounted point with all Alluxio users. Note that this configuration has no effect on HDFS nor local UFS.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.object.store.use.put.object.stream.file.length
records.description: When the write length is less than or equal to the configured value, we will use the putObject stream to write the data.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.object.store.directory.priority.over.file
records.description: When it is set to true and there are file and directory with the same name in UFS, only the directory is displayed and the file is hidden.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.object.store.log.object.not.found
records.description: If enabled, Alluxio will log a warning message when a file is not found in the object storage under file system during read operations.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.object.store.iterative.list.dedup.v2
records.description: If enabled, alluxio will use new logic to handle object storage iterative listing dedup. This fixed the previous listing bug where some keys are missing during listing.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.eventual.consistency.retry.base.sleep
records.description: To handle eventually consistent storage semantics for certain under storages, Alluxio will perform retries when under storage metadata doesn't match Alluxio's expectations. These retries use exponential backoff. This property determines the base time for the exponential backoff.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.eventual.consistency.retry.max.num
records.description: To handle eventually consistent storage semantics for certain under storages, Alluxio will perform retries when under storage metadata doesn't match Alluxio's expectations. These retries use exponential backoff. This property determines the maximum number of retries. This property defaults to 0 as modern object store UFSs provide strong consistency.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.eventual.consistency.retry.max.sleep
records.description: To handle eventually consistent storage semantics for certain under storages, Alluxio will perform retries when under storage metadata doesn't match Alluxio's expectations. These retries use exponential backoff. This property determines the maximum wait time in the backoff.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.business.retry.base.sleep
records.description: To handle some occasional error in business for certain under storages, Alluxio will perform retries. These retries use exponential backoff. This property determines the base time for the exponential backoff.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.business.retry.max.num
records.description: To handle transient errors when accessing the under storage (e.g. S3 503 SlowDown / 429 throttling), Alluxio retries the failed operation with exponential backoff. This property determines the maximum number of retries. Defaults to 3; set to 0 to disable retry.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.business.retry.max.sleep
records.description: To handle some occasional error in business for certain under storages, Alluxio will perform retries. when under storage metadata doesn't match Alluxio's expectations. These retries use exponential backoff. This property determines the maximum wait time in the backoff.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.oss.connection.max
records.description: The maximum number of OSS connections.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.oss.connection.timeout
records.description: The timeout when connecting to OSS.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.oss.connection.ttl
records.description: The TTL of OSS connections in ms.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.oss.socket.timeout
records.description: The timeout of OSS socket.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.oss.ecs.ram.role
records.description: The RAM role of current owner of ECS.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.oss.retry.max
records.description: The maximum number of OSS error retry.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.oss.proxy.host
records.description: The proxy host address for OSS connection, if any.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.oss.proxy.port
records.description: The proxy port for OSS connection, if any.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.oss.sts.ecs.metadata.service.endpoint
records.description: The ECS metadata service endpoint for Aliyun STS

records.name: alluxio.underfs.oss.sts.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable oss STS(Security Token Service).

records.name: alluxio.underfs.oss.sts.token.refresh.interval.ms
records.description: Time before an OSS Security Token is considered expired and will be automatically renewed

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.admin.threads.max
records.description: The maximum number of threads to use for metadata operations when communicating with S3. These operations may be fairly concurrent and frequent but should not take much time to process.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.disable.dns.buckets
records.description: Optionally, specify to make all S3 requests path style.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.endpoint
records.description: Optionally, to reduce data latency or visit resources which are separated in different AWS regions, specify a regional endpoint to make aws requests. An endpoint is a URL that is the entry point for a web service. For example, s3.cn-north-1.amazonaws.com.cn is an entry point for the Amazon S3 service in beijing region.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.endpoint.region
records.description: Optionally, set the S3 endpoint region. If not provided, inducted from the endpoint uri or set to null

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.owner.id.to.username.mapping
records.description: Optionally, specify a preset s3 canonical id to Alluxio username static mapping, in the format "id1=user1;id2=user2". The AWS S3 canonical ID can be found at the console address https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/home?#security_credential . Please expand the "Account Identifiers" tab and refer to "Canonical User ID". Unspecified owner id will map to a default empty username

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.proxy.host
records.description: Optionally, specify a proxy host for communicating with S3.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.proxy.port
records.description: Optionally, specify a proxy port for communicating with S3.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.region
records.description: Optionally, set the S3 bucket region. If not provided, will enable the global bucket access with extra requests

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.sdk.version
records.description: Specify the version of AWS S3 SDK version.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.force.global.bucket.access.enabled
records.description: Set this property to false by default to disable global bucket access.Only if the region and endpoint are not configured, the global bucket access is enabled. Set this property to true to enabled global bucket access in all cases.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.connections.max
records.description: The maximum number of concurrent connections to S3, including both connections for uploading data and performing metadata operations. This number should be at least the sum of the maximum admin threads and maximum upload threads.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.upload.threads.max
records.description: For an Alluxio worker, this is the maximum number of threads to use for uploading data to S3 for multipart uploads. These operations can be fairly expensive, so multiple threads are encouraged. However, this also splits the bandwidth between threads, meaning the overall latency for completing an upload will be higher for more threads. For the Alluxio master, this is the maximum number of threads used for the rename (copy) operation. It is recommended that value should be greater than or equal to

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.default.mode
records.description: Mode (in octal notation) for S3 objects if mode cannot be discovered.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.directory.suffix
records.description: Directories are represented in S3 as zero-byte objects named with the specified suffix.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.inherit.acl
records.description: Set this property to false to disable inheriting bucket ACLs on objects. Note that the translation from bucket ACLs to Alluxio user permissions is best effort as some S3-like storage services doe not implement ACLs fully compatible with S3.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.intermediate.upload.clean.age
records.description: Streaming uploads or multipart uploads may not have been completed/aborted correctly and need periodical ufs cleanup. If ufs cleanup is enabled, intermediate multipart uploads in all non-readonly S3 mount points older than this age will be cleaned. This may impact other ongoing upload operations, so a large clean age is encouraged.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.list.objects.v1
records.description: Whether to use version 1 of GET Bucket (List Objects) API.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.list.objects.skip.empty.start.after
records.description: Whether to omit the start-after parameter from S3 ListObjectsV2 requests when its value is empty. AWS S3 tolerates an empty start-after, but some S3-compatible stores (e.g. Baidu BOS) treat it as a reset-to-prefix-start signal, causing recursive listings to loop on page 1. Set to false to restore the previous behavior of always sending start-after.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.max.error.retry
records.description: The maximum number of retry attempts for failed retryable requests.Setting this property will override the AWS SDK default.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.request.timeout
records.description: The timeout for a single request to S3. Infinity if set to 0. Setting this property to a non-zero value can improve performance by avoiding the long tail of requests to S3. For very slow connections to S3, consider increasing this value or setting it to 0.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.secure.http.enabled
records.description: Whether or not to use HTTPS protocol when communicating with S3.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.secure.http.trust.all.certs
records.description: Whether to trust all certificate when communicating with S3 in HTTPS.

records.name: alluxio.underfs.s3.server.side.encryption.enabled
records.description: Whether or not to encrypt data stored in S3.

records.description: The load job total load failure count threshold. -1 means never fail.

records.name: alluxio.master.dora.load.job.retry.dlq.capacity
records.description: The capacity of the dead letter queue we persist failed tasks in memory for retrying purpose. Once the queue is full, failed subtasks will not retryand will just fail. On average one subtask takes up 0.5KB in memory. Properly set this property to avoid OOM.

records.name: alluxio.master.dora.load.job.subtask.max.retry.attempts
records.description: Maximum retry attempts per load subtask before it is marked as permanently failed. Increase to better tolerate transient worker overload such as RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED rejections during high-concurrency loads on a small worker pool. Set to 1 to restore the legacy single-retry behavior. Read at master startup; requires a master restart to apply.

records.name: alluxio.master.dora.load.job.failed.file.list.dir
records.description: The directory to store failed file list of a distributed load job.

records.name: alluxio.master.bind.host
records.description: The hostname that Alluxio master binds to.

records.name: alluxio.master.metastore.dir
records.description: The metastore work directory. Only some metastores need disk.

records.name: alluxio.master.metastore.dir.job
records.description: If the metastore is ROCKS, this property controls where the RocksDB stores job metadata. This property defaults to . And it can be used to change job metadata storage path to a different disk to improve RocksDB performance.

records.name: alluxio.master.metastore.rocks.exclusive.lock.timeout
records.description: Before RocksDB is shut down/restarted/restored, Master will wait for ongoing operations to complete/abort. This timeout specifies how long to wait before forcing the action. Then the leftover operations will fail. Normally the wait will be short, because when master fails over/shuts down/replays journal, all other concurrent operations should have been stopped. This is just one extra safety guard. Therefore we do not recommend setting this manually.

records.name: alluxio.master.metastore.rocks.parallel.backup
records.description: Whether to checkpoint rocksdb in parallel using the number of threads set by %s.

records.name: alluxio.master.metastore.rocks.parallel.backup.threads
records.description: The number of threads used by backing up rocksdb in parallel.

records.name: alluxio.master.network.max.inbound.message.size
records.description: The maximum size of a message that can be sent to the Alluxio master

records.name: alluxio.master.network.flowcontrol.window

records.name: alluxio.master.network.keepalive.time
records.description: The amount of time for Alluxio master gRPC server to wait for a response before pinging the client to see if it is still alive.

records.name: alluxio.master.network.keepalive.timeout
records.description: The maximum time for Alluxio master gRPC server to wait for a keepalive response before closing the connection.

records.name: alluxio.master.network.permit.keepalive.time
records.description: Specify the most aggressive keep-alive time clients are permitted to configure. The server will try to detect clients exceeding this rate and when detected will forcefully close the connection.

records.name: alluxio.master.heartbeat.timeout
