records.description: Whether to run UFS operations with managed blocking. This will provide RPC layer a hint that UFS is possible slow.The default is true for object stores and false for the rest. unless set explicitly.

records.name: alluxio.master.web.bind.host
records.description: The hostname Alluxio master web UI binds to.

records.name: alluxio.master.web.hostname
records.description: The hostname of Alluxio Master web UI.

records.name: alluxio.master.web.port
records.description: The port Alluxio web UI runs on.

records.name: alluxio.master.worker.connect.wait.time
records.description: Alluxio master will wait a period of time after start up for all workers to register, before it starts accepting client requests. This property determines the wait time.

records.name: alluxio.master.scheduler.etcd.read.timeout
records.description: Timeout for Etcd read operations in the scheduler.

records.name: alluxio.master.scheduler.etcd.write.timeout
records.description: Timeout for Etcd write operations in the scheduler.

records.name: alluxio.master.scheduler.etcd.max.retries
records.description: Maximum number of retries for Etcd operations in the scheduler.

records.name: alluxio.master.scheduler.etcd.retry.base.delay
records.description: Base delay for exponential backoff when retrying Etcd operations.

records.name: alluxio.master.scheduler.etcd.global.reconciliation.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable decentralized global orphaned-job reconciliation. When enabled, each coordinator periodically scans for jobs owned by dead coordinators and moves them back to WAITING for re-scheduling. Disable this if external tooling handles orphan recovery.

records.name: alluxio.master.scheduler.sync.job.timeout
records.description: Timeout for synchronous job execution in the scheduler.

records.name: alluxio.master.scheduler.running.job.capacity
records.description: The maximum number of jobs running on the scheduler.

records.name: alluxio.master.scheduler.job.etcd.poll.interval
records.description: The interval at which the scheduler polls for new jobs from the waiting queue on Etcd. A shorter interval enables faster job distribution but increases Etcd load

records.name: alluxio.master.scheduler.job.etcd.path
records.description: Specifies the Etcd path prefix where load jobs are stored. This prefix is used to organize and store job-related metadata on Etcd

records.name: alluxio.master.scheduler.load.job.etcd.endpoints
records.description: A comma-separated list of Etcd endpoints for load job distribution. If not set, it defaults to the global Etcd endpoints (alluxio.etcd.endpoints).

records.name: alluxio.master.scheduler.waiting.job.capacity
records.description: The maximum number of jobs wait on the scheduler.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.scheduler.fast.refill.enabled
records.description: If true, the coordinator refills a job's per-worker tasks up to x active workers (x the disorder tolerance) instead of a fixed 3, giving higher concurrency and slow-node tolerance on the distributed-load path.

records.name: alluxio.master.disorder.task.tolerance.percentage
records.description: The coordinator's scheduling tolerance will be adjusted.Scheduling will be paused once the number of slow tasks exceeds this tolerance.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.task.logging.threshold
records.description: When the task execution time exceeds the threshold,the call log is recorded.

records.name: alluxio.master.scheduler.max.tasks.per.worker
records.description: The maximum number of tasks scheduler can schedule simultaneously on each worker.

records.name: alluxio.master.worker.info.cache.refresh.time
records.description: The worker information list will be refreshed after being cached for this time period. If the refresh time is too big, operations on the job servers or clients may fail because of the stale worker info. If it is too small, continuously updating worker information may case lock contention in the block master

records.name: alluxio.master.rpc.executor.type
records.description: Type of ExecutorService for Alluxio master gRPC server. Supported values are TPE (for ThreadPoolExecutor) and FJP (for ForkJoinPool).

records.name: alluxio.master.rpc.executor.core.pool.size
records.description: The number of threads to keep in thread pool of master RPC ExecutorService.

records.name: alluxio.master.rpc.executor.max.pool.size
records.description: The maximum number of threads allowed for master RPC ExecutorService. When the maximum is reached, attempts to replace blocked threads fail.

records.name: alluxio.master.rpc.executor.keepalive
records.description: The keep alive time of a thread in master RPC ExecutorServicelast used before this thread is terminated (and replaced if necessary).

records.name: alluxio.master.rpc.executor.tpe.queue.type
records.description: This property is effective when %s is set to TPE. It specifies the internal task queue that's used by RPC ExecutorService. Supported values are: LINKED_BLOCKING_QUEUE, ARRAY_BLOCKING_QUEUE and SYNCHRONOUS_BLOCKING_QUEUE

records.name: alluxio.master.rpc.executor.tpe.queue.size
records.description: This property is effective when %s is set to TPE and %s is set to one of the bounded queue types: ,. It specifies the size of the internal task queue that's used by RPC ExecutorService.

records.name: alluxio.master.rpc.executor.tpe.allow.core.threads.timeout
records.description: This property is effective when %s is set to ThreadPoolExecutor. It controls whether core threads can timeout and terminate when there is no work.

records.name: alluxio.master.rpc.executor.fjp.parallelism
records.description: This property is effective when %s is set to ForkJoinPool. It controls the parallelism level (internal queue count) of master RPC ExecutorService.

records.name: alluxio.master.rpc.executor.fjp.min.runnable
records.description: This property is effective when %s is set to ForkJoinPool. It controls the minimum allowed number of core threads not blocked. A value of 1 ensures liveness. A larger value might improve throughput but might also increase overhead.

records.name: alluxio.master.rpc.executor.fjp.async
records.description: This property is effective when %s is set to ForkJoinPool. if true, it establishes local first-in-first-out scheduling mode for forked tasks that are never joined. This mode may be more appropriate than default locally stack-based mode in applications in which worker threads only process event-style asynchronous tasks.

records.name: alluxio.master.worker.register.lease.enabled
records.description: Whether workers request for leases before they register. The RegisterLease is used by the master to control the concurrency of workers that are actively registering.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.global.file.index.enabled
records.description: If enabled, the coordinator will maintain the global file index and keep in sync during load/free/rebalance.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.global.file.index.type
records.description: The global file index metadata type (rocks/heap)

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.rebalance.job.worker.timeout
records.description: The worker timeout of the rebalance job.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.rebalance.job.history.max.retained
records.description: The maximum number of finished rebalance jobs retained in etcd. When a new rebalance job is launched, older finished jobs beyond this limit are deleted, oldest first. A value of 0 or less disables pruning (unbounded history). Each retained job record is roughly 5-10 KB for a small membership change and up to about 1-2 MB for a large replace across many workers; the retained history occupies approximately this count multiplied by the per-job size in etcd.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.scheduler.sync.job.threads.max
records.description: The number of workers that can register at the same time. Others will wait and retry until they are granted a RegisterLease. If you observe pressure on the master when many workers start up and register, tune down this parameter.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.job.meta.store.type
records.description: The job metadata type (rocks/etcd)

records.name: alluxio.quota.enabled
records.description: Whether quota feature is enabled

records.name: alluxio.quota.etcd.polling.interval.ms
records.description: Interval for polling ETCD for the latest quota definitions. By default the interval is 3s.

records.name: alluxio.quota.limit.exceeded.action
records.description: The action to take when usage under a quota limit is exceeded.

records.name: alluxio.quota.coordinator.heartbeat.threads
records.description: The number of threads in the Coordinator to poll and manage worker quota usage.

records.name: alluxio.quota.worker.heartbeat.interval.ms
records.description: The master polls all workers for the latest quota usage regularly. This property specifies the interval between two polls.

records.name: alluxio.quota.worker.heartbeat.timeout.ms
records.description: The master polls all workers for the latest quota usage regularly. This property specifies the worker needs to respond the quota usage within this timeout.

records.name: alluxio.quota.evictor.capacity.modifier.size
records.description: This configuration tweaks the behavior of the evictor which restores quota usage. A positive value like 64MB means the evictor will try to free 64MiB ahead of time on each worker. A negative value like -1MB means the evictor will tolerate each worker to use 1MB more than the capacity. The default value is 1MB, which means after eviction, the quota usage will still be slightly more than the quota definition, so the quota stays full.

records.name: alluxio.stream.consistency.check.enabled
records.description: When this is enabled, Alluxio guarantees data consistency for the life cycle of a data stream. If the file contents changes during an ongoing stream, the stream will fail. And the caller should retry reading the whole file.

records.name: alluxio.stream.consistency.check.update.worker.cache
records.description: When this is enabled, if a data stream fails on observing data inconsistency, the client will best-effort notify all relevant workers about a potential data version change. Consequently, the cluster will drop out-of-date cache and the next read will successfully observe latest file contents.

records.name: alluxio.ttl.policy.enabled
records.description: Whether TTL policy feature is enabled

records.name: alluxio.ttl.policy.etcd.polling.interval.ms
records.description: Interval for polling ETCD for the latest TTL policy definitions. By default the interval is 3s.

records.name: alluxio.master.filesystem.liststatus.result.message.length
records.description: Count of items on each list-status response message.

records.name: alluxio.priority.eviction.rule.etcd.polling.interval
records.description: Interval for polling ETCD for the latest priority rules. By default the interval is 3s.

records.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.bind.device
records.description: The device name Alluxio's worker rpc service binds to.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.data.bind.device
records.description: The RDMA device name (e.g. 'mlx5_0:1') that Alluxio's RDMA transport should use for both the UCX listener and data transfer. Device names can be obtained via 'ibv_devinfo' (format: device_name:port_number). When a single device is specified, the corresponding network interface IP is auto-resolved for the UCX listener bind address and registered as the RDMA connect host. Multiple devices can be specified as a comma-separated list (e.g. 'mlx5_0:1,mlx5_1:1'); in that case the listener falls back to alluxio.network.rdma.data.bind.host. If unset, UCX will auto-select all available devices.

records.name: alluxio.worker.data.bind.device
records.description: The device name Alluxio's worker Netty data service binds to.

records.name: alluxio.worker.rest.bind.device
records.description: The device name Alluxio's worker rest service binds to.

records.name: alluxio.worker.web.bind.device
records.description: The device name Alluxio's worker web binds to.

records.name: alluxio.worker.bind.host
records.description: The hostname Alluxio's worker node binds to.

records.name: alluxio.worker.block.heartbeat.interval
records.description: The interval between block workers' heartbeats to update block status, storage health and other workers' information to Alluxio Master.

records.name: alluxio.worker.container.hostname
records.description: The container hostname if worker is running in a container.

records.name: alluxio.worker.data.folder
records.description: A relative path within each storage directory used as the data folder for Alluxio worker to put data for tiered store.

records.name: alluxio.worker.data.ufs.read.rate.limit.mb
records.description: The maximum bandwidth for ufs reading per second. If 0 or unset then no rate limit is enforced.

records.name: alluxio.cluster.rate.limit.enabled
records.description: Whether the cluster-wide per-user FUSE rate limiter is enabled. When false the limiter is not constructed and adds zero overhead on the I/O path.

records.name: alluxio.cluster.rate.limit.server.port
records.description: The port the cluster rate-limit token server listens on (coordinator side) and that FUSE token clients connect to.

records.name: alluxio.cluster.rate.limit.fallback.to.local
records.description: When the rate-limit token server is unreachable, whether each FUSE client falls back to enforcing the limit locally (fail-open). When false, requests block until the server is reachable again.

records.name: alluxio.cluster.rate.limit.batch.fraction
records.description: Fraction of a tenant's per-second limit that each FUSE client fetches from the token server per refill (one RPC amortized across the batch). Larger values mean fewer refill RPCs and lower stall risk (favoring throughput) but let a client hold more unused tokens locally (looser cross-client accuracy); smaller values are tighter but chattier. The result is clamped to the configured min/max batch size and never exceeds the per-second limit itself.

records.name: alluxio.cluster.rate.limit.batch.min.size
records.description: Lower bound on the per-refill token batch a FUSE client fetches from the rate-limit token server.

records.name: alluxio.cluster.rate.limit.batch.max.size
records.description: Upper bound on the per-refill token batch a FUSE client fetches from the rate-limit token server. Caps how many tokens a single client can hold locally, bounding cross-client inaccuracy at the cost of more refill RPCs at very high limits.

records.name: alluxio.worker.data.folder.permissions
records.description: The permission set for the worker data folder.

records.name: alluxio.worker.data.server.domain.socket.address
records.description: The path to the domain socket. In this case, it makes use of a UNIX domain socket when this is set (non-empty). This is a special path in the file system that allows the client and the AlluxioWorker to communicate. You will need to set a path to this socket. The AlluxioWorker needs to be able to create the path. If is set, the path should be the home directory for the domain socket. The full path for the domain socket with be {path}/{uuid}.

records.name: alluxio.worker.data.load.buffer.size
records.description: The read buffer size for the buffered (non-streaming) distributed-load path (see ={@code false}): one UFS read covers this many bytes and is held in direct memory while its pages are inserted. If less than the page size it is raised to the page size. Unused by the streaming path, which reads one page at a time from a single per-partition stream.

records.name: alluxio.worker.data.load.streaming.enabled
records.description: If true (default), distributed load reads each file through a single per-partition stream (one ranged GET per partition on object stores) and inserts it one page at a time, so the UFS read granularity is decoupled from resident memory: direct memory is one page per concurrent load task, independent of the GET size. This is the same path for all file sizes. If false, falls back to the legacy buffered path, which reads up to per UFS read and holds it in direct memory while inserting its pages. Both produce identical on-disk results; set to false as a per-worker rollback knob.

records.name: alluxio.worker.load.file.partition.size
records.description: If set to a value greater than 0, during the distributed load, worker will partition the file into small slices and load them concurrently. Please consider turn on this feature if the file size is large but the file amount is small.

records.name: alluxio.worker.meta.iterator.batch.size
records.description: Batch size for iterating file metadata in DoraMeta when using DoraMetaReadOnlyBatchIterator

records.name: alluxio.worker.data.server.domain.socket.as.uuid
records.description: If true, the property is the path to the home directory for the domain socket and a unique identifier is used as the domain socket name. If false, the property is the absolute path to the UNIX domain socket.

records.name: alluxio.worker.rebalance.max.parallel.worker.sessions
records.description: The max number of parallel worker-worker data transfer sessions during worker rebalance initiated by each worker.

records.name: alluxio.worker.rebalance.max.parallel.files.per.worker
records.description: The max number of parallel load files during a worker-worker data transfer

records.name: alluxio.worker.rebalance.fast.file.scan
records.description: Whether or not to use fast file list scan implementation

records.name: alluxio.worker.startup.timeout
records.description: Maximum time to wait for worker startup.

records.name: alluxio.worker.file.buffer.size
records.description: The buffer size for worker to write data into the tiered storage.

records.name: alluxio.worker.free.space.timeout
records.description: The duration for which a worker will wait for eviction to make space available for a client write request.

records.name: alluxio.worker.hostname
records.description: The hostname of Alluxio worker.

records.name: alluxio.worker.identity.external
records.description: The unique identity string of the worker. Worker instances in a Alluxio cluster must have different identities. This overrides and should be used with discretion

records.name: alluxio.worker.identity.uuid.file.path
records.description: The path to the file containing the identity of the worker specified as a UUID. This path should be readable and writable to the worker process. Worker instances in a Alluxio cluster must have different identities.

records.name: alluxio.worker.master.connect.retry.timeout
records.description: Retry period before workers give up on connecting to master and exit.

records.name: alluxio.worker.master.periodical.rpc.timeout
records.description: Timeout for periodical RPC between workers and the leading master. This property is added to prevent workers from hanging in periodical RPCs with previous leading master during flaky network situations. If the timeout is too short, periodical RPCs may not have enough time to get response from the leading master during heavy cluster load and high network latency.

records.name: alluxio.worker.ramdisk.size
records.description: The allocated memory for each worker node's ramdisk(s). It is recommended to set this value explicitly.

records.name: alluxio.worker.job.load.executor.threads.max
records.description: The maximum number of threads used to read files in the data server. Increasing this value allows more concurrent read operations but increases memory and CPU overhead per worker.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.grpc.writer.threads.max
records.description: The maximum number of threads used to write files in the data server.

records.name: alluxio.ds.worker.copy.threads.max
records.description: Maximum number of copy thread pools in data shuttle.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.ufs.reader.threads.max
records.description: The maximum number of threads used to load files from UFS.

records.name: alluxio.worker.async.page.persistence.enabled
records.description: If enabled, worker will persist loaded pages into page store asynchronously.

records.name: alluxio.worker.async.page.persistence.threads.max
records.description: The maximum number of threads used to persist loaded pages to page store.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.max.page.number.limit.enabled
records.description: If enabled, worker will evict pages if the number of cached pages exceed a threshold. Set to specify this limit on the number of cached pages.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.max.page.number
records.description: The maximal number of pages to be cached.

records.name: alluxio.worker.loading.from.ufs.dedup.enabled
records.description: If enabled, will do dedup when multiple cold read requests try reading the same page from UFS. There'll be only one thread loading the data and others will block until the load finishes.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.writer.buffer.size.messages
records.description: When a client writes to a remote worker, the maximum number of data messages to buffer by the server for each request.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.reader.threads.core
records.description: The number of core threads used to read pages in the netty data server.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.reader.threads.max
records.description: The maximum number of threads used to read pages in the netty data server.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.reader.thread.keep.alive.time
records.description: Idle keep-alive time for non-core threads in the netty reader pool.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.reader.queue.size
records.description: The size of the queue in the reader thread pool

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.reader.queue.type
records.description: Backing queue for the netty reader thread pool. HANDOFF_QUEUE pairs with BoundedHandoffExecutor; other values use a plain ThreadPoolExecutor.

The size of the task queue shared by the load, write, and copy job thread pools. Tasks are handed off directly to threads (synchronous) when threads are available; the queue absorbs bursts when all threads are busy. Increase this to buffer more tasks at the cost of higher latency signaling.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.queue.size
records.description: The size of the queue in the writer thread pool. Shared by both the writer and UFS-writer pools.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.queue.type
records.description: Backing queue for the netty writer thread pool. HANDOFF_QUEUE pairs with BoundedHandoffExecutor; other values use a plain ThreadPoolExecutor.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.ufs.writer.queue.type
records.description: Backing queue for the netty UFS-writer thread pool. HANDOFF_QUEUE pairs with BoundedHandoffExecutor; other values use a plain ThreadPoolExecutor. Queue size (when bounded) comes from .

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.queue.enabled
records.description: Whether netty buffer queue is enabled

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.threads.core
records.description: The number of core threads used to write pages in the netty data server. Shared by both the writer and UFS-writer pools.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.threads.max
records.description: The maximum number of threads used to write pages in the netty data server.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.thread.keep.alive.time
records.description: Idle keep-alive time for non-core threads in the netty writer pool. Shared by both the writer and UFS-writer pools.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.ufs.writer.threads.max
records.description: The maximum number of threads used to write files to UFS in the netty data server.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.buffer.size.packets
records.description: The maximum number of parallel data packets when a client writes to a worker.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.read.handler.thread.idle.timeout
records.description: The maximum time a worker keeps a handler thread ready for listening to client requests on an open channel. If no requests occur within this time, the idle thread is released. Shorter values improve thread utilization but may incur a scheduling overhead when a new request comes in after the thread has been released.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.reader.buffer.size.packets
records.description: The maximum number of parallel data packets when a client reads from a worker.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.read.watchdog.enabled
records.description: Whether the worker periodically logs in-flight Netty read requests that have been stuck in a non-terminal state longer than . Logging only; helps diagnose reads that never terminate (and so never emit a close log).

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.read.watchdog.stuck.threshold
records.description: How long an in-flight Netty read request may make no progress (no state transition) before the watchdog reports it as wedged. Defaults to roughly twice (default 10s) — a read stalled longer than the client's per-response timeout, twice over. The scan interval is derived from this value, so there is no separate interval to configure.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.flowcontrol.window
records.description: The HTTP2 flow control window used by worker gRPC connections. Larger value will allow more data to be buffered but will use more memory.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.keepalive.time
records.description: The amount of time for data server (for block reads and block writes) to wait for a response before pinging the client to see if it is still alive.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.keepalive.timeout
records.description: The maximum time for a data server (for block reads and block writes) to wait for a keepalive response before closing the connection.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.permit.keepalive.time
records.description: Specify the most aggressive keep-alive time clients are permitted to configure. The server will try to detect clients exceeding this rate and when detected will forcefully close the connection.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.max.inbound.message.size
records.description: The max inbound message size used by worker gRPC connections.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.boss.threads
records.description: How many threads to use for accepting new requests.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.channel
records.description: Netty channel type: NIO or EPOLL. If EPOLL is not available, this will automatically fall back to NIO.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.file.transfer
records.description: When returning files to the user, select how the data is transferred; valid options are `MAPPED` (uses java MappedByteBuffer) and `TRANSFER` (uses Java FileChannel.transferTo).

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.shutdown.quiet.period
records.description: The quiet period. When the netty server is shutting down, it will ensure that no RPCs occur during the quiet period. If an RPC occurs, then the quiet period will restart before shutting down the netty server.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.watermark.high
records.description: Determines how many bytes can be in the write queue before switching to non-writable.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.watermark.low
records.description: Once the high watermark limit is reached, the queue must be flushed down to the low watermark before switching back to writable.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.file.region.backpressure.enabled
records.description: If true, count FileRegion bytes toward the netty data-server's outbound buffer watermark, so WriteBufferWaterMark backpressure bounds per-channel FileRegion queue depth. Bounds the worker's open-FD count per in-flight S3 GET regardless of object size. Disable only as an emergency rollback if the size-estimator change interacts badly with a specific workload; revert restores the previous behaviour where FileRegions are treated as zero-sized and the outbound queue can grow with response size.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.worker.threads
records.description: Number of threads to use for processing requests in worker

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.reader.buffer.size
records.description: When a client reads from a remote worker, the maximum amount of data not received by client allowed before the worker pauses sending more data. If this value is lower than read chunk size, read performance may be impacted as worker waits more often for buffer to free up. Higher value will increase the memory consumed by each read request.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.reader.buffer.pooled
records.description: Whether it is using pooled direct buffer or unpooled wrapped buffer when creating a buffer for remote read

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.reader.max.chunk.size.bytes
records.description: When a client read from a remote worker, the maximum chunk size.

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.shutdown.timeout
records.description: Maximum amount of time to wait until the worker gRPC server is shutdown (regardless of the quiet period).

records.name: alluxio.worker.network.zerocopy.enabled
records.description: Whether zero copy is enabled on worker when processing data streams.

records.name: alluxio.worker.remote.io.slow.threshold
records.description: The time threshold for when a worker remote IO (read or write) of a single buffer is considered slow. When slow IO occurs, it is logged by a sampling logger.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.async.eviction.enabled
records.description: If this is enabled, the page cache will be evicted asynchronously when the space reaches the high water level threshold. And then it will keep clearing the page cache until low water level threshold is reached.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.async.eviction.check.interval
records.description: The interval for checking if page cache async eviction is needed.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.async.eviction.high.watermark
records.description: The high water mark threshold used for page cache asynchronous eviction.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.async.eviction.low.watermark
records.description: The low water mark threshold used for page cache asynchronous eviction.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.async.restore.enabled
records.description: If this is enabled, restore pages asynchronously.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.async.write.enabled
records.description: If this is enabled, cache data asynchronously.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.async.write.threads
records.description: Number of threads to asynchronously cache data.

records.name: alluxio.worker.file.meta.extraction.dir
records.description: A directory where file-level metadata files in rocksdb are exported.

records.name: alluxio.worker.outdated.parquet.scan.period
records.description: Interval for cleaning outdated file-level metadata parquet file

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.dirs
records.description: A list of the directories where pages in paged block store are stored.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.eviction.retries
records.description: Max number of eviction retries.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.evictor.class
records.description: Deprecated. Please use instead.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.evictor.type
records.description: The strategy that worker uses to evict local cached pages when running out of space. Currently valid options include

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.evictor.lfu.logbase
records.description: The log base for client cache LFU evictor bucket index.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.evictor.nondeterministic.enabled
records.description: If this is enabled, the evictor picks uniformly from the worst k elements.Currently only LRU is supported.

records.name: alluxio.priority.eviction.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable eviction by priority. Higher priority pages won't be evicted until all lower priority pages have been evicted.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.local.store.file.buckets
records.description: The number of file buckets for the page blocked store on local file system. It is recommended to set this to a high value if the number of unique files is expected to be high (# files / file buckets <= 100,000).

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.pinned.file.capacity.limit.ratio
records.description: Set the ratio of how much space pinned files can occupy with regard to the total capacity.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.overhead
records.deprecated_message: use instead and specify an absolute value alluxio.worker.page.store.reserved.size
records.description: Deprecated. Use instead. A fraction value representing the storage overhead writing to disk. For example, with 1GB allocated cache space, and 10% storage overhead we expect no more than 1024MB / (1 + 10%) user data to store.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.reserved.size
records.description: Size of the reserved space for each cache directory of the worker's page store, not included in the sizes specified by . This reserved space is used for purposes like buffering temporary pages before they are done writing. Default size is min(100GB, capacity * 10%).

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.sync.restore.max.blocking.timeout
records.description: The max time that synchronous page restoration will block worker startup, when is disabled. When the timeout is reached, the worker will resume the startup sequence, while continue to restore any remaining pages in the background.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.page.size
records.description: Size of each page in worker paged block store.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.parallel.restore.enabled
records.description: Whether to restore the pages in parallel when the worker starts up.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.sizes
records.description: A list of maximum cache size for each cache directory.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.split.file.into.multiple.directories
records.description: Whether the pages of a file will stored in multiple cache directories, instead of in a single directory.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.timeout.duration
records.description: The timeout duration for local cache I/O operations (reading/writing/deleting). When this property is a positive value,local cache operations after timing out will fail and fallback to external file system but transparent to applications; when this property is a negative value, this feature is disabled.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.timeout.threads
records.description: The number of threads to handle cache I/O operation timeout, when is positive.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.type
records.description: The type of page store to use for worker page store. Can be either `LOCAL` or `MEM`. The `LOCAL` page store stores all pages in a directory, the `MEM` page store stores all pages in memory.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.startup.compatibility.scan
records.description: The compatibility scan will verify the running Alluxio version canwork with the existing page store cache. If not compatible,the worker will perform a full scan on the cache and migrate themto the new format. This full scan, if triggered, will take some time to completeso the worker takes more time to start. It can be turned off if there isno compatibility concerns or under instructions.

records.name: alluxio.worker.page.store.read.external.logging.threshold
records.description: When reading an external page exceeds the configured time, a log message will be printed.

records.name: alluxio.worker.preload.data.thread.pool.size
records.description: The worker preload data thread pool size; Each thread loads a page.

records.name: alluxio.worker.preload.huggingface.max.concurrency
records.description: The max number of concurrent threads used to download a single Huggingface model file.

records.name: alluxio.worker.preload.data.thread.pool.queue.capacity
records.description: The worker preload queue size. When the queue is full, new tasks will be rejected.

records.name: alluxio.worker.preload.task.expiration.ttl
records.description: The worker preload task expiration TTL.Early jobs are ignored to box the wait queue size.

records.name: alluxio.worker.register.lease.enabled
records.description: Whether the worker requests a lease from the master before registering.This should be consistent with

records.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.port
records.description: The port for Alluxio worker's RPC service.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.data.bind.host
records.description: The hostname that the Alluxio RDMA data server runs on.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.data.hostname
records.description: The hostname of the Alluxio RDMA data service that clients use to connect to.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.data.port
records.description: The port for the Alluxio RDMA data service.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.metadata.port
records.description: The port for the Alluxio RDMA metadata service.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.metadata.enabled
records.description: Whether the worker RDMA metadata service is enabled. When false, the worker metadata transport server will not start and clients will not use the worker metadata transport path.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.metadata.device
records.description: The RDMA device used by the metadata service. When unset, the metadata transport chooses its default active device.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.metadata.ib.port
records.description: The RDMA IB port used by the metadata service.

records.name: alluxio.network.rdma.metadata.gid.index
records.description: The RDMA GID index used by the metadata service. Negative means the metadata transport chooses its default GID.

records.name: alluxio.worker.data.bind.host
records.description: The hostname that the Alluxio worker's data server runs on. See multi-homed networks.

records.name: alluxio.worker.data.hostname
records.description: The hostname of Alluxio worker data service.

records.name: alluxio.worker.data.port
records.description: The port Alluxio's worker's data server runs on.

records.name: alluxio.worker.web.bind.host
records.description: The hostname Alluxio worker's web server binds to.

records.name: alluxio.worker.web.hostname
records.description: The hostname Alluxio worker's web UI binds to.

records.name: alluxio.worker.web.port
records.description: The port Alluxio worker's web UI runs on.

records.name: alluxio.worker.rest.port
records.description: The default HTTP port for Alluxio worker's s3 api.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.https.port
records.description: The HTTPs port for Alluxio worker's s3 api.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.only.https.access
records.description: Set to true to only provide the HTTPs port and disable the default HTTP port for s3 api

records.name: alluxio.worker.ufs.block.open.timeout
records.description: Timeout to open a block from UFS.

records.name: alluxio.worker.ufs.instream.cache.enabled
records.description: Enable caching for seekable under storage input stream, so that subsequent seek operations on the same file will reuse the cached input stream. This will improve position read performance as the open operations of some under file system would be expensive. The cached input stream would be stale, when the UFS file is modified without notifying alluxio.

records.name: alluxio.worker.ufs.instream.cache.expiration.time
records.description: Cached UFS instream expiration time.

records.name: alluxio.worker.ufs.instream.cache.max.size
records.description: The max entries in the UFS instream cache.

records.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.type
records.description: Type of ExecutorService for Alluxio worker gRPC server. Supported values are TPE (for ThreadPoolExecutor) and FJP (for ForkJoinPool).

records.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.core.pool.size
records.description: The number of threads to keep in thread pool of worker RPC ExecutorService.

records.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.max.pool.size
records.description: The maximum number of threads allowed for worker RPC ExecutorService. When the maximum is reached, attempts to replace blocked threads fail.

records.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.keepalive
records.description: The keep alive time of a thread in worker RPC ExecutorServicelast used before this thread is terminated (and replaced if necessary).

records.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.tpe.queue.type
records.description: This property is effective when %s is set to TPE. It specifies the internal task queue that's used by RPC ExecutorService. Supported values are: LINKED_BLOCKING_QUEUE, ARRAY_BLOCKING_QUEUE and SYNCHRONOUS_BLOCKING_QUEUE

records.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.tpe.queue.size
records.description: This property is effective when %s is set to TPE and %s is set to one of the bounded queue types: ,. It specifies the size of the internal task queue that's used by RPC ExecutorService.

records.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.tpe.allow.core.threads.timeout
records.description: This property is effective when %s is set to ThreadPoolExecutor. It controls whether core threads can timeout and terminate when there is no work. Must be false when %s is HANDOFF_QUEUE, which pre-starts a core worker to keep the pool warm.

records.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.fjp.parallelism
records.description: This property is effective when %s is set to ForkJoinPool. It controls the parallelism level (internal queue count) of master RPC ExecutorService.

records.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.fjp.min.runnable
records.description: This property is effective when %s is set to ForkJoinPool. It controls the minimum allowed number of core threads not blocked. A value of 1 ensures liveness. A larger value might improve throughput but might also increase overhead.

records.name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.fjp.async
records.description: This property is effective when %s is set to ForkJoinPool. if true, it establishes local first-in-first-out scheduling mode for forked tasks that are never joined. This mode may be more appropriate than default locally stack-based mode in applications in which worker threads only process event-style asynchronous tasks.

records.name: alluxio.worker.membership.manager.type
records.description: Type of membership manager used for workers.Choose STATIC for pre-configured members.Choose ETCD for using etcd for membership managementDefault is MASTER which does not enable membership module and uses master to register

records.name: alluxio.worker.membership.manager.auto.commit.after.registration
records.description: Whether the worker auto commits the changes after it registers to membership manager after startup, when is enabled

records.name: alluxio.worker.metrics.etcd.sync.interval
records.description: The interval at which a worker syncs its capacity metrics (used bytes and capacity bytes) to etcd via the membership entity.

records.name: alluxio.worker.failure.detection.timeout
records.description: The timeout to consider a worker failure in membership failure detection.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.failure.detection.timeout
records.description: The timeout to consider a coordinator failure in membership failure detection.

records.name: alluxio.worker.static.membership.manager.config.file
records.description: Absolute path of the config file for listof worker hostnames/IPs for the cluster. needs to be set to STATIC first.

records.name: alluxio.worker.open.file.handle.clean.up.ttl
records.description: A TTL that determines how long an open file handle can remain idle before it is automatically reclaimed. This setting is used to clean up stale file handles.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.access.logging.enabled
records.description: Set to true to enable worker netty s3 access log.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.api.enabled
records.description: Set to true to enable worker netty s3 server.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.rest.api.compatible.enabled
records.description: Set to true to enable the partial RESTful API on worker s3 server.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.selective.api.metrics.enabled
records.description: Set to true to enable request metrics collection for S3 API calls. Use %s to specify the list of API methods that metrics will be collected for.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.selective.api.metrics.list
records.description: A comma-separated list of Alluxio S3 API method names that request metrics will be collected for, e.g. "GetObject,HeadObject,ListObjects"

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.processing.enabled
records.description: If enabled, S3 requests are dispatched off the Netty event loop into the async light/heavy thread pools. Keeping work off the event loop prevents a single slow request from wedging all keep-alive connections pinned to that loop. Disable only for diagnostic purposes.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.light.pool.core.thread.number
records.description: Core thread number for async light thread pool. Defaults to the same value as the maximum thread number so that ThreadPoolExecutor never calls Thread.start() from the caller (event loop) thread on the hot path.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.light.pool.maximum.thread.number
records.description: Maximum thread number for async light thread pool.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.light.pool.queue.size
records.description: Queue size for async light thread pool. Bounded so that bursts above sustainable load surface as RejectedExecutionException back-pressure rather than unbounded queue growth.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.heavy.pool.backpressure.timeout
records.description: Maximum time a heavy-pool thread will wait for the Netty channel to become writable (backpressure) before aborting with an IOException. Prevents heavy-pool threads from being pinned indefinitely by hung or slow TCP clients. Set to 0 to disable the timeout (not recommended).

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.heavy.pool.core.thread.number
records.description: Core thread number for async heavy thread pool. Defaults to the same value as the maximum thread number so that ThreadPoolExecutor never calls Thread.start() from the caller (event loop) thread on the hot path. Sized to absorb concurrent HEAVY-tag S3 ops (GetObject, PutObject, CopyObject, UploadPartCopy) under high connection counts (~512 MB stack).

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.heavy.pool.maximum.thread.number
records.description: Maximum thread number for async heavy thread pool. Keep this >= peak concurrent S3 connections handling HEAVY ops; under-sizing surfaces as s3_api_async_rejections{kind="heavy"} and 503 SlowDown.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.heavy.pool.queue.size
records.description: Queue size for async heavy thread pool. Bounded so that bursts above sustainable load surface as RejectedExecutionException back-pressure rather than unbounded queue growth.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.prefetch.max.part.number
records.description: Maximum number of prefetched parts the worker-side S3 prefetch pool keeps ahead per active read stream. Combined with this caps the lookahead window per stream.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.prefetch.part.length
records.description: Bytes per prefetched part on the worker-side S3 prefetch pool. Default 4 MiB matches the pre-PR worker behaviour (via the client FAST pool); smaller parts reduce first-byte latency on cold reads at the cost of more S3 GETs per prefetched window.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.prefetch.threads.max
records.description: Maximum number of threads in the worker-side S3 prefetch pool. Used by S3RangeReader for response prefetch and by S3NettyObjectTask for asynchronous full-page warming on sub-page reads. Submissions to this pool are bursty (one per S3 GET in the worst case); size accordingly.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.prefetch.queue.size
records.description: Capacity of the bounded fallback queue used by the worker-side S3 prefetch pool once all threads are busy. Submissions beyond this bound are rejected; the foreground UFS read still serves the request.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.audit.logging.enabled
records.description: Set to true to enable worker netty s3 audit.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.audit.logging.queue.capacity
records.description: Capacity of the queue used by the worker S3 audit logging writer.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.authentication.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable check s3 rest request header.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.authenticator.classname
records.description: The class's name is instantiated as an S3 authenticator.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.authentication.session.provider
records.description: The session provider type for S3 authentication.OIDC: the session token is generated by OIDC provider.ALLUXIO_STS: use temporary session token generated by Alluxio Security Token Service.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.privileged.users.credentials
records.description: A JSON object mapping accessKey to secretKey for privileged users, e.g., '{"accessKey1":"secretKey1","accessKey2":"secretKey2"}'.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.signature.validation.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable S3 signature validation for S3 requests. It is only used when is true, and is set to alluxio.s3.auth.AlluxioIamAuthenticator.

records.name: alluxio.worker.sts.signature.validation.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable STS signature validation for STS requests. It is only used when is true, and is set to alluxio.s3.auth.AlluxioIamAuthenticator.

records.name: alluxio.security.sts.callers.credentials
records.description: A JSON object mapping accessKey to secretKey for STS callers authentication, e.g., '{"accessKey1":"secretKey1","accessKey2":"secretKey2"}'.

records.name: alluxio.security.sts.token.key
records.description: A key for internal Alluxio use only. This key is used by the stateless token engine to encrypt and sign Session Tokens, ensuring the confidentiality and integrity of the tokens. This key must never be disclosed to any external client.

records.name: alluxio.security.authentication.token.external.jwksaddr
records.description: The HTTPS JWKS endpoint or JSON file for retrieving keys to validate JWT tokens. This is used when the token is signed by an external authority, and the keys are not stored in Alluxio.

records.name: alluxio.security.authentication.service.token.path
records.description: Specifies the path to the service token file, used for authentication when starting a service, worker/coordinator/security service/CLI.

records.name: alluxio.security.authentication.token.default.duration
records.description: Default lifetime of a temporary session token. The internal refreshed token has the same lifetime.

records.name: alluxio.security.authentication.token.max.duration
records.default: "43200sec") // 12 hours .setDescription("Maximum lifetime of a temporary session token."
records.description: Maximum lifetime of a temporary session token.

records.name: alluxio.security.authentication.token.allowed.clock.skew
records.description: Allowed clock skew when verifying token timestamps.This is used to account for any time differences betweenthe token issuer and Alluxio services.

records.name: alluxio.security.authentication.token.aud
records.description: The audience claim in the token, used to verify the token.If set, the token must contain an 'aud' claim that matches this value.

records.name: alluxio.security.authentication.token.tid
records.description: The token ID claim in the token, used to verify the token.If set, the token must contain a 'tid' claim that matches this value.

records.name: alluxio.security.authentication.token.nbf.check
records.description: Whether to check the 'not before' claim in the token. If true, the token is not valid before the 'nbf' time.

records.name: alluxio.security.authentication.token.assume.user.field
records.description: Custom field in the token as the requested authentication user.if set, the value of this field is used to specify the Alluxio username.

records.name: alluxio.security.authentication.token.assume.group.field
records.description: Custom field in the token as the requested authentication groups.if set, the value of this field is used to specify the Alluxio groups.

records.name: alluxio.security.authentication.token.assume.role.field
records.description: Custom field in the token as the requested authentication role.if set, the value of this field is used to specify the Alluxio role.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.authorization.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable check s3 rest request header.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.authorizer.classname
records.description: The class's name is instantiated as an S3 authorizer.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.bucketpathcache.timeout
records.description: Per-worker TTL for the positive cache that records whether an Alluxio S3 bucket path exists and is a directory. Disabled by default ("0min") to match AWS S3 read-after-write consistency for bucket lifecycle. The cache is consulted by the bucket-existence checks that remain on the bucket-level metadata path (HeadBucket / GetBucketLocation / GetBucketVersioning / ListMultipartUploads / DeleteBucket and the few remaining checkPathIsAlluxioDirectory call sites in object-level operations); enabling it (e.g. "60s") trades a bounded staleness window for fewer bucket-validate RPCs on those paths. Only positive (exists) entries are cached — missing buckets are not negatively cached so a freshly-created bucket isn't masked until TTL expiry. The 404-disambiguation helpers in NettyRestUtils do not consult this cache; they always re-probe.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.bucket.naming.restrictions.enabled
records.description: Toggles whether or not the Alluxio S3 API will enforce AWS S3 bucket naming restrictions. See https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/bucketnamingrules.html.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.list.directory.as.object.enabled
records.description: Toggles whether the Alluxio S3 API will include directories as 0-byte objects in ListObjects/ListObjectsV2 responses. When true (the default), every directory encountered during listing is returned as a 0-byte entry in Contents (Alluxio's historical behavior). When false, the prefix path itself is suppressed and synthesized parent folders (folders with no backing UFS object, identified by a zero lastModificationTimeMs) are dropped, while real 0-byte directory marker objects (folders carrying a positive mtime plumbed through from the underlying object's LastModified) are preserved. Listings with a delimiter are unaffected: directories continue to surface via CommonPrefixes.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.complete.multipart.upload.min.part.size
records.description: The minimum required file size of parts for multipart uploads. Parts which are smaller than this limit aside from the final part will result in an EntityTooSmall error code. Set to 0 to disable size requirements.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.connection.keep.alive.enabled
records.description: If enabled the connection keep-alive, S3 API will maintain the TCP connection to support the client to reuse it and send new requests.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.connection.idle.max.time
records.description: Specify the max keep-alive time for the HTTP connection in S3 API. If the channel has been idle for the specified time, the connection will be closed. If set it as 0, the connection will not actively expire.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.deletetype
records.description: Delete type when deleting buckets and objects through S3 API. Valid options are `%s` (delete both in Alluxio and UFS), `%s` (delete only the buckets or objects in Alluxio namespace).

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.header.metadata.max.size
records.description: The maximum size to allow for user-defined metadata in S3 PUTrequest headers. Set to 0 to disable size limits.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.only.read.cache.data.enabled
records.description: If enabled, readObject request can only read data when the data are cached in Alluxio. If the data are not cached, it will return the not-found error.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.redirect.enabled
records.description: If enabled, readObject request may redirect the request to another appropriate server based on the consistent hash ring and cache load situation.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.write.redirect.enabled
records.description: If enabled, write requests may be redirected to another appropriate server based on the consistent hash ring and cache load situation.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.redirect.health.check.enabled
records.description: When enabled, an RPC is used to verify the health of the worker before redirecting to S3. Note: This will introduce significant overhead in both latency and throughput.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.tagging.restrictions.enabled
records.description: Toggles whether or not the Alluxio S3 API will enforce AWS S3 tagging restrictions (10 tags, 128 character keys, 256 character values) See https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/tagging-managing.html.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.receive.packet.size
records.description: Packet size for each split chunk of the s3 request.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.transfer.packet.size
records.description: Packet size for each transferred and flushed data.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.tls.enabled
records.description: Set it to true to enable the TLS on S3 API.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.writetype
records.description: Write type when creating buckets and objects through S3 API. Currently, only `CACHE_THROUGH` (try to cache, write to UnderFS synchronously), and `THROUGH` (no cache, write to UnderFS synchronously). are supported

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.put.object.calculate.md5.enabled
records.description: Whether calculate md5 on S3 API.
The type of cache to use for parquet data, either ONHEAP or OFFHEAP. Footer cache is always on-heap.
The type of cache size to use for parquet data, either ENTRY or MEMSIZE.

records.name: alluxio.worker.parquet.max.object.size
records.description: Sets the maximum object graph size (number of objects) Ehcache will traverse during sizeof calculation for Parquet Raw cache entries. This helps prevent traversing very large object graphs when estimating cache size.

records.name: alluxio.worker.parquet.raw.footer.cache.size
records.description: Parquet Raw API footer cache size in number of entries

records.name: alluxio.worker.parquet.raw.index.cache.size
records.description: Parquet Raw API index cache size in number of entries

records.name: alluxio.worker.parquet.raw.offset.cache.size
records.description: Parquet Raw API offset cache size in number of entries

records.name: alluxio.worker.parquet.raw.footer.cache.mem.size
records.description: Parquet Raw API footer cache size in bytes

records.name: alluxio.worker.parquet.raw.index.cache.mem.size
records.description: Parquet Raw API index cache size in bytes

records.name: alluxio.worker.parquet.raw.offset.cache.mem.size
records.description: Parquet Raw API offset cache size in bytes

records.name: alluxio.worker.parquet.raw.prefetch.offset
records.description: When this property is true, all the offset index for a particular column will be prefetched when it is first accessed

records.name: alluxio.worker.parquet.raw.prefetch.offset.async
records.description: If true, the preloading of parquet offset cache is asynchronous. Otherwise, it will be synchronous.

records.name: alluxio.worker.parquet.offset.preload.pool.size
records.description: The maximum number of threads in the thread pool for preloading Parquet offset indexes. This is used to speed up random reads of Parquet files.

records.name: alluxio.worker.parquet.offset.preload.queue.size
records.description: The maximum queue size in the thread pool for preloading Parquet offset indexes. This is used to speed up random reads of Parquet files.

records.name: alluxio.user.block.worker.client.pool.min
records.description: The minimum number of block worker clients cached in the block worker client pool.

records.name: alluxio.user.block.worker.client.pool.max
records.description: The maximum number of block worker clients cached in the block worker client pool.

records.name: alluxio.user.block.worker.client.pool.gc.threshold
records.description: A block worker client is closed if it has been idle for more than this threshold.

records.name: alluxio.user.conf.sync.interval
records.description: The time period of client master heartbeat to update the configuration if necessary from meta master.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.replication.max
records.description: The target max replication level of a file in Alluxio space. Setting this property to a negative value means no upper limit.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.replication.min
records.description: The target min replication level of a file in Alluxio space.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.replication.durable
records.description: The target replication level of a file created by ASYNC_THROUGH writesbefore this file is persisted.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.target.media
records.description: Preferred media type while storing file's blocks.

records.name: alluxio.user.block.size.bytes.default
records.description: Default block size for Alluxio files.

records.name: alluxio.user.conf.cluster.default.enabled
records.description: When this property is true, an Alluxio client will load the default values of cluster-wide configuration and path-specific configuration set by Alluxio master.

records.name: alluxio.user.date.format.pattern
records.description: Display formatted date in cli command and web UI by given date format pattern.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.buffer.bytes
records.description: The size of the file buffer to use for file system reads/writes.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.reserved.bytes
records.description: The size to reserve on workers for file system writes.Using smaller value will improve concurrency for writes smaller than block size.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.delete.unchecked
records.description: Whether to check if the UFS contents are in sync with Alluxio before attempting to delete persisted directories recursively.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.master.client.pool.size.min
records.description: The minimum number of fs master clients cached in the fs master client pool. For long running processes, this should be set to zero.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.master.client.pool.size.max
records.description: The maximum number of fs master clients cached in the fs master client pool.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.master.client.pool.gc.interval
records.description: The interval at which file system master client GC checks occur.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.master.client.pool.gc.threshold
records.description: A fs master client is closed if it has been idle for more than this threshold.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.metadata.load.type
records.description: The behavior of loading metadata from UFS. When information about a path is requested and the path does not exist in Alluxio, metadata can be loaded from the UFS. Valid options are `ALWAYS`, `NEVER`, and `ONCE`. `ALWAYS` will always access UFS to see if the path exists in the UFS. `NEVER` will never consult the UFS. `ONCE` will access the UFS the "first" time (according to a cache), but not after that. This parameter is ignored if a metadata sync is performed, via the parameter "alluxio.user.file.metadata.sync.interval"

records.name: alluxio.user.file.metadata.refresh.ahead.margin.ms
records.description: The time margin between when to refresh a file's metadata and when the snapshot of the file's metadata expires. The ahead-of-time refresh will reduce the chance when the snapshot has not yet expired, but is very close to expiry, and will probably expire considering the network delay and queuing times, etc. when a request is processed by the worker.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.metadata.sync.interval
records.description: The interval for syncing UFS metadata before invoking an operation on a path. -1 means no sync will occur. 0 means Alluxio will always sync the metadata of the path before an operation. If you specify a time interval, Alluxio will (best effort) not re-sync a path within that time interval. Syncing the metadata for a path must interact with the UFS, so it is an expensive operation. If a sync is performed for an operation, the configuration of "alluxio.user.file.metadata.load.type" will be ignored.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.metadata.real.content.hash
records.description: Whether to get real content hash of file when the file is loaded from UFS.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.passive.cache.enabled
records.description: Whether to cache files to local Alluxio workers when the files are read from remote workers (not UFS).

records.name: alluxio.user.file.readtype.default
records.description: Default read type when creating Alluxio files. Valid options are `CACHE_PROMOTE` (move data to highest tier if already in Alluxio storage, write data into highest tier of local Alluxio if data needs to be read from under storage), `CACHE` (write data into highest tier of local Alluxio if data needs to be read from under storage), `NO_CACHE` (no data interaction with Alluxio, if the read is from Alluxio data migration or eviction will not occur).

records.name: alluxio.user.file.persist.on.rename
records.description: Whether or not to asynchronously persist any files which have been renamed. This is helpful when working with compute frameworks which use rename to commit results.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.persistence.initial.wait.time
records.description: Time to wait before starting the persistence job. When the value is set to -1, the file will be persisted by rename operation or persist CLI but will not be automatically persisted in other cases. This is to avoid the heavy object copy in rename operation when %s is set to %s. This value should be smaller than the value of %s

records.name: alluxio.user.file.segment.enabled
records.description: Whether to enabled segmented read of files, possibly from multiple workers. If enabled, read requests will be split into segments, and data is pulled from multiple workers. Note this currently only works with read-only workloads.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.segment.size
records.description: Size of a file segment that will be cached and read independently on multiple workers. Note for a given file, all clients must agree on a common segment size, otherwise they will make incompatible decisions about where to store and retrieve the segments of the file. Segment size should be a multiplier of

records.name: alluxio.user.file.waitcompleted.poll
records.description: The time interval to poll a file for its completion status when using waitCompleted.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.create.ttl
records.description: Time to live for files created by a user, no ttl by default.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.create.ttl.action
records.description: When file's ttl is expired, the action performs on it. Options: FREE(default), DELETE_ALLUXIO or DELETE

records.name: alluxio.user.block.read.metrics.enabled
records.description: Whether detailed block read metrics will be recorded and sink.

records.name: alluxio.user.block.avoid.eviction.policy.reserved.size.bytes
records.description: The portion of space reserved in a worker when using the LocalFirstAvoidEvictionPolicy class as block location policy.

records.name: alluxio.user.client.block.worker.pool.resource.ttl
records.description: The block worker client TTL created by the client pool. If the TTL expires, the resource will be recycled.

records.name: alluxio.user.client.block.worker.pool.acquire.timeout
records.description: The block worker client pool timeout for acquiring a resource.

When this property is a positive value,local cache operations after timing out will fail and fallback to external file system but transparent to applications; when this property is a negative value, this feature is disabled.

records.name: alluxio.user.client.cache.timeout.threads
records.description: The number of threads to handle cache I/O operation timeout, when is positive.

records.name: alluxio.user.client.cache.store.overhead
records.description: A fraction value representing the storage overhead writing to disk. For example, with 1GB allocated cache space, and 10% storage overhead we expect no more than 1024MB / (1 + 10%) user data to store.

records.name: alluxio.user.client.cache.store.type
records.description: The type of page store to use for client-side cache. Can be either `LOCAL` or `ROCKS`. The `LOCAL` page store stores all pages in a directory, the `ROCKS` page store utilizes rocksDB to persist the data.

records.name: alluxio.user.client.cache.local.store.file.buckets
records.description: The number of file buckets for the local page store of the client-side cache. It is recommended to set this to a high value if the number of unique files is expected to be high (# files / file buckets <= 100,000).

records.name: alluxio.user.client.cache.local.store.put.staging.enabled
records.description: If true, LocalPageStore.put() writes the page to a staging file under the cache dir's .staging/ subdirectory and atomically renames it into place. Preserves snapshot consistency for in-flight POSIX-pinned zero-copy reads when the same page is concurrently rewritten (e.g. passive cache / re-load), and prevents leaving a 0-byte stub at the page path if the disk hits ENOSPC mid-write. If false, falls back to in-place O_TRUNC + write. Default true; disable only to mitigate a regression specific to staging-based writes.

records.name: alluxio.user.client.cache.local.store.direct.io.enabled
records.description: If true, the local page store writes staged pages with direct I/O (O_DIRECT), bypassing the OS page cache. Because the page store is itself a cache, buffered writes double-cache the data and pollute the page cache; direct I/O avoids that and reduces the CPU spent copying data into the kernel page cache, at the cost of an extra user-space copy into an aligned buffer. Only the staging write path is affected; metadata and append writes stay buffered. Direct I/O is only supported on Linux filesystems that allow O_DIRECT (e.g. ext4, xfs); on an unsupported filesystem the staged page write fails and the error is surfaced, so disable this option there. Applies to both the worker page store and the client-side cache.

records.name: alluxio.user.client.cache.local.store.direct.io.min.size
records.description: Minimum staged page write size for using direct I/O. When alluxio.user.client.cache.local.store.direct.io.enabled is true, writes smaller than this threshold use the buffered write path instead. Set to 0 to allow direct I/O for all staged page writes.

If true, direct I/O is restricted to write-cache pages, which come from page-aligned pooled buffers and benefit most from O_DIRECT; all other pages (e.g. read-cache fills) use the buffered write path. If false, the file id is not checked and any page may take the direct I/O path. Only has an effect when alluxio.user.client.cache.local.store.direct.io.enabled is true.

records.name: alluxio.user.client.unmounted.ufs.fallback.enabled
records.description: By default, given the path to be accessed, if it is not in the mount table, a MountPointNotFoundRuntimeException will be thrown. If this is enabled, client side will try to create an under file system instance and fallback to UFS, even if the path. is not in the mount table.

records.description: If enabled, the consistent hash ring will be explicitly versioned to support batching updates and commiting afterwards.

records.name: alluxio.user.consistent.hash.ring.versioning.max.history.retained
records.description: Max number of history versions of the hash ring retained in etcd.

records.name: alluxio.user.multiplexed.worker.client.enabled
records.description: When set to true, this property enables optimized gRPC communication between client and workers, leveraging gRPC built-in functions to multiplex connections and handle inactive resources.

records.name: alluxio.user.default.delete.all.replicas
records.description: If true, the client will attempt to find all replicas of a file and delete them. Otherwise, the client will only delete from the main worker for the path according to consistent hash. This configuration is recommended if Alluxio is running with multiple replicas for each file.

records.name: alluxio.user.worker.selection.policy.ketama.hash.replicas
records.description: This is the value of replicas in the ketama hashing algorithm. When workers changes, it will guarantee the hash table is changed only in a minimal. The value of replicas should be X times the physical nodes in the cluster, where X is a balance between efficiency and cost.

records.name: alluxio.user.worker.selection.policy.maglev.hash.lookup.size
records.description: This is the size of the lookup table in the maglev hashing algorithm. It must be a prime number. In the maglev hashing, it will generate a lookup table for workers. The bigger the size of the lookup table, the smaller the variance of this hashing algorithm will be. But bigger look up table will consume more time and memory

records.name: alluxio.user.worker.selection.policy.multi.probe.hash.probe.num
records.description: This is the number of probes in the multi-probe hashing algorithm. In the multi-probe hashing algorithm, the bigger the number of probes, the smaller the variance of this hashing algorithm will be. But more probes will consume more time and memory

records.name: alluxio.user.file.writetype.default
records.description: Default write type when creating Alluxio files. Valid options are `CACHE_THROUGH` (try to cache, write to UnderFS synchronously), `THROUGH` (no cache, write to UnderFS synchronously)

records.name: alluxio.user.hostname
records.description: The hostname to use for an Alluxio client.

records.name: alluxio.user.file.write.tier.default
records.description: The default tier for choosing a where to write a block. Valid option is any integer. Non-negative values identify tiers starting from top going down (0 identifies the first tier, 1 identifies the second tier, and so on). If the provided value is greater than the number of tiers, it identifies the last tier. Negative values identify tiers starting from the bottom going up (-1 identifies the last tier, -2 identifies the second to last tier, and so on). If the absolute value of the provided value is greater than the number of tiers, it identifies the first tier.

records.name: alluxio.user.worker.selection.policy
records.description: Deprecated. Use instead.

records.name: alluxio.user.worker.selection.policy.type
records.description: The policy a client uses to map a file path to a worker address. The only option is Other options are for internal tests only and not for real deployments.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.worker.selection.policy.type
records.description: Optional override of applied only when selecting workers for the write cache path. When unset, the write cache reuses the read-side policy. Set to to spread writes toward workers with the most remaining pinned-cache capacity.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.pinned.capacity.policy.top.fraction
records.description: Used by the write-cache worker selection policy. The fraction [0, 1] of workers, ranked by remaining pinned-cache capacity, that always stay in the candidate pool (set to 0 to disable the floor and rely solely on the inclusion threshold). The emptiest workers up to this fraction are kept even when most of the cluster is filling up, so writes concentrate on the least-full nodes under pressure.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.pinned.capacity.policy.inclusion.percent
records.description: Used by the write-cache worker selection policy. Any worker whose remaining pinned-cache capacity is above this percentage is always kept in the candidate pool, in addition to the top fraction. This lets a worker keep receiving writes until it is (100 minus this value) percent full, so a lightly loaded cluster spreads writes across every worker instead of parking the lowest-ranked ones.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.pinned.capacity.policy.min.remaining.percent
records.description: Used by the write-cache worker selection policy. Workers whose remaining pinned-cache capacity is below this percentage are dropped from the candidate pool (lowest-ratio first), but the pool is never reduced below the requested replica count.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.dir.stream.cache.size
records.description: Number of child entries cached during a directory listing

records.name: alluxio.fuse.fs.name
records.description: The FUSE file system name.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.use.create.file.options.overwrite.flag
records.description: When this configuration is set to true, Fuse will use the overwrite flag in createFileOptions when overwriting files instead of directly deleting the file, which helps reduce the number of metadata requests.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.request.hard.timeout

records.name: alluxio.fuse.return.timeout.error.on.request.hard.timeout

records.name: alluxio.fuse.logging.threshold

records.name: alluxio.fuse.max.reader.concurrency

records.name: alluxio.fuse.read.write.lock.manager.try.lock.timeout

records.name: alluxio.fuse.mount.alluxio.path
