records.name: alluxio.fuse.mount.options
records.description: The platform specific Fuse mount options to mount the given Fuse mount point. If multiple mount options are provided, separate them with comma.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.mount.point
records.description: The absolute local filesystem path that mount Alluxio path to.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.umount.timeout
records.description: The timeout to wait for all in progress file read and write to finish before unmounting the Fuse filesystem when SIGTERM signal is received. A value smaller than or equal to zero means no umount wait time.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.list.timeout
records.description: The timeout to wait for list dir. A value smaller than or equal to zero means wait for readdir until finished.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.low.level.active.inode.attr.cache.ttl
records.description: The maximum age of a retained low-level active inode snapshot that can be returned directly from the low-level FUSE active inode table without refreshing metadata from the backend. A value smaller than or equal to zero disables this userspace inode attribute cache.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.user.group.translation.enabled
records.description: Whether to translate Alluxio users and groups into Unix users and groups when exposing Alluxio files through the FUSE API. When this property is set to false, the user and group for all FUSE files will match the user who started the alluxio-fuse process.Note that this applies to JNR-FUSE only.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.authorization.user.cache.expiration.time
records.description: The expiration time for the cached user authorization info in FUSE.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.authorization.user.cache.max.size
records.description: The maximum number of users to cache authorization info for in FUSE.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.special.command.enabled
records.description: If enabled, user can issue special FUSE commands by using 'ls -l /path/to/fuse_mount/.alluxiocli.<command_name>.<subcommand_name>', For example, when the Alluxio is mounted at local path /mnt/alluxio-fuse, 'ls -l /mnt/alluxio-fuse/.alluxiocli.metadatacache.dropAll' will drop all the user metadata cache. 'ls -l /mnt/alluxio-fuse/.alluxiocli.metadatacache.size' will get the metadata cache size， the size value will be show in the output's filesize field. 'ls -l /mnt/alluxio-fuse/path/to/be/cleaned/.alluxiocli.metadatacache.drop' will drop the metadata cache of path '/mnt/alluxio-fuse/path/to/be/cleaned/'
Whether to enable distributed POSIX locks in FUSE V2. Requires alluxio.write.cache.dual.buffer.file.system.type to be GENERIC_FDB_BACKED_V2.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.fast.copy.enabled
records.description: If enabled, ignore chmod and chown for fast copying.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.enable.read.dir.plus
records.description: If enabled, the fuse will read dir by offset.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.read.dir.plus.batch.size

records.name: alluxio.fuse.enforce.direct.io.read

records.name: alluxio.fuse.local.backed.stream.tmp.dir.capacity
records.description: The maximum disk usage for FUSE local backed stream.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.local.backed.stream.enabled

records.name: alluxio.fuse.local.backed.stream.buffer.size
records.description: This buffer size is used in FuseLocalBackedStream when copying the UFS file to local and copying the local file to UFS.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.open.file.reference.creation.wait.previous.close.timeout
records.description: Timeout for creating open file reference in the open file registry, when there is a previous close operation on the same file path in progress.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.silly.rename.interceptor.enabled
records.description: Manually manages the 'silly rename' behavior in FUSE. By enabling this, Alluxio intercepts rename requests that would create '.fuse_hidden' files. It enforces immediate deletion of the original file during a rename and ignores (no-op) any explicit delete calls on existing '.fuse_hidden' files to prevent unnecessary metadata operations.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.open.read.status.cache.enabled
records.description: If enabled, caches file metadata (URIStatus) for 1 second to avoid repeated getStatus RPCs when the same read-only file is opened frequently. Only used for read-only file opens, not for getattr.

records.description: The port Alluxio client data runs on.

records.name: alluxio.user.network.data.bind.device
records.description: The device name Alluxio's client binds to.

records.description: Whether enable fuse non-disruptive migration.

records.name: alluxio.fuse.migration.ongoing.request.grace_period

records.name: alluxio.security.authentication.custom.provider.class
records.description: The class to provide customized authentication implementation, when alluxio.security.authentication.type is set to CUSTOM. It must implement the interface 'alluxio.security.authentication.AuthenticationProvider'.

records.name: alluxio.security.client.authentication.type
records.description: The authentication mode for client. Currently two modes are supported: NOSASL, OIDC. The default value NOSASL indicates that no authentication is enabled.

records.name: alluxio.security.authentication.type
records.description: The authentication mode. Currently three modes are supported: NOSASL, SIMPLE, CUSTOM. The default value NOSASL indicates that no authentication is enabled. Server trusts whoever the client claims to be.

records.name: alluxio.security.authorization.permission.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable access control based on file permission.

records.name: alluxio.security.authorization.permission.supergroup
records.description: The super group of Alluxio file system. All users in this group have super permission.

records.name: alluxio.security.authorization.permission.umask
records.description: The umask of creating file and directory. The initial creation permission is 777, and the difference between directory and file is 111. So for default umask value 022, the created directory has permission 755 and file has permission 644.

records.name: alluxio.security.group.mapping.cache.timeout
records.description: Time for cached group mapping to expire.

records.name: alluxio.security.group.mapping.class
records.description: The class to provide user-to-groups mapping service. Master could get the various group memberships of a given user. It must implement the interface 'alluxio.security.group.GroupMappingService'. The default implementation execute the 'groups' shell command to fetch the group memberships of a given user.

records.name: alluxio.security.login.impersonation.username
records.description: When %s is set to SIMPLE or CUSTOM, user application uses this property to indicate the IMPERSONATED user requesting Alluxio service. If it is not set explicitly, or set to %s, impersonation will not be used. A special value of '%s' can be specified to impersonate the hadoop client user.

records.name: alluxio.security.login.username
records.description: When alluxio.security.authentication.type is set to SIMPLE or CUSTOM, user application uses this property to indicate the user requesting Alluxio service. If it is not set explicitly, the OS login user will be used.

records.name: alluxio.security.stale.channel.purge.interval
records.description: Interval for which client channels that have been inactive will be regarded as unauthenticated. Such channels will reauthenticate with their target master upon being used for new RPCs.

records.name: alluxio.network.tls.ssl.context.provider.classname
records.description: Full name of the class that will be instantiated for providing SSL contexts.

records.name: alluxio.network.tls.enabled
records.description: If true, enables TLS on all network communication between all Alluxio clients, masters, and workers.

records.name: alluxio.network.tls.keystore.alias
records.description: When the keystore contains multiple keys/aliases, this parameter must be set with the alias name of the key to use for the server. If the keystore only contains a single key, this parameter is ignored.

records.name: alluxio.network.tls.keystore.path
records.description: The path to the keystore (in JKS format) for the server side ofa TLS connection.

records.name: alluxio.network.tls.keystore.password
records.description: The password for the keystore.

records.name: alluxio.network.tls.keystore.key.password
records.description: The password for the key in the keystore.

records.name: alluxio.network.tls.server.protocols
records.description: Comma-separated list of protocol names to enable on the server. If unset, the default internal list is used, which typically includes all the supported protocols. Example: TLSv1.1,TLSv1.2

records.name: alluxio.network.tls.truststore.path
records.description: The path to the truststore (in JKS format) for the client side of a TLS connection.

records.name: alluxio.network.tls.truststore.password
records.description: The password for the truststore.

records.name: alluxio.network.tls.client.no.endpoint.identification
records.description: If true, don't verify the server's hostname against the Common Name (CN) or Subject Alternative Name (SAN) in the server's certificate. This is not recommended as it makes the connection insecure.

records.name: alluxio.network.tls.ca.cert
records.description: The path to the CA certificate file for TLS connections. This is used to verify the server certificate when the client connects to the server. If this is not set, the client will not verify the server certificate, which is not recommended. This is also used by the server to verify the client certificate.

records.name: alluxio.network.tls.server.cert
records.description: The path to the server certificate file for TLS connections. This is used by the server to present its certificate to the client. If this is not set, the server will not present a certificate, which is not recommended.

records.name: alluxio.network.tls.server.key.password
records.description: The password for the server key file for TLS connections. This is used by the server to present its key to the client. If this is not set, the server will not present a key, which is not recommended.

records.name: alluxio.worker.s3.tls.mutual.enabled
records.description: Set it to true to enable the mutual TLS on S3 API.

records.name: alluxio.job.max.concurrent.capacity
records.description: The maximum concurrency of all current jobs.

records.name: alluxio.job.batch.size
records.description: The number of tasks would be included in a job request.

records.name: alluxio.job.cleanup.interval
records.description: The interval for the periodic cleanup of finished jobs from the job metastore. This process removes jobs that are in a terminal state (e.g., SUCCEEDED, FAILED, STOPPED) to prevent the metastore from growing indefinitely with historical job records.

records.name: alluxio.job.retention.time
records.description: The length of time the Alluxio should save information about completed jobs before they are discarded.

records.name: alluxio.job.get.worker.cached.bytes.timeout
records.description: The timeout that job get worker cached bytes.

records.name: alluxio.job.ufs.status.pool.min.size
records.description: The min size of loading ufs status pool.

records.name: alluxio.job.ufs.status.pool.max.size
records.description: The max size of loading ufs status pool.

records.name: alluxio.job.load.sub.task.retry.timeout
records.description: The timeout for a load subtask to be retried.

records.name: alluxio.job.load.partition.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable partitioned reads of files during load. A partition is a contiguous subset of a file loaded independently by a worker.

records.name: alluxio.job.load.partition.size
records.description: Size of a file partition during load.

records.name: alluxio.load.job.without.quota.allowed
records.description: If the quota not exist, the job skip quota check.

records.name: alluxio.access.logging.enabled
records.description: Whether or not cache access logs are enabled, which records internal cache accesses for further analysis.

records.name: alluxio.access.logging.poll.interval
records.description: The interval at which the access logs are asynchronously flushed to disk.

records.name: alluxio.audit.logging.enabled
records.description: Whether or not system audit logs are enabled, which records operations on Alluxio's northbound FUSE/REST/S3 interfaces.

records.name: alluxio.multi.cluster.enabled
records.description: If enabled, alluxio client is able to connect to multiple clusters

records.name: alluxio.multi.cluster.config.path
records.description: The alluxio multi cluster config path file

records.name: alluxio.foundationdb.external.client.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable FDB multi-threaded client networking. When enabled, uses the native client library directory as the external client directory to allow multiple network threads.

records.name: alluxio.foundationdb.client.threads.per.version
records.description: Number of network threads per FDB client library version. Only effective when external client directory is configured. 0 means auto-detect (availableProcessors / 4, minimum 2).

records.name: alluxio.foundationdb.transaction.timeout
records.description: Default transaction timeout in milliseconds for FDB transactions. 0 means no timeout. Note that FDB still enforces a 5-second read version staleness window — transactions that take longer than 5s from their read version will fail with transaction_too_old regardless of this setting.

records.name: alluxio.foundationdb.transaction.retry.limit
records.description: Maximum number of retries for FDB transactions on conflict. -1 means unlimited retries (FDB default).

records.name: alluxio.etcd.endpoints
records.description: A list of comma-separated http://host:port addresses of etcd cluster (e.g. http://localhost:2379,http://etcd1:2379)

records.name: alluxio.etcd.num.retries
records.description: Number of max retries when communicating with Etcd.

records.name: alluxio.etcd.max.inbound.message.size
records.description: The maximum inbound message size for Etcd client.

records.name: alluxio.etcd.username
records.description: Username for communication with Etcd.Make sure the given user has the full readwrite role permission on all keys with prefix '/'. Refer to etcd official site for Authentication section.

records.name: alluxio.etcd.password
records.description: User password for communication with Etcd.

records.name: alluxio.etcd.tls.ca.cert
records.description: CA cert file for communication with Etcd.

records.name: alluxio.etcd.tls.client.cert
records.description: Client cert file for communication with Etcd.

records.name: alluxio.etcd.tls.client.key
records.description: Client key file for communication with Etcd.

records.name: alluxio.etcd.tls.client.key.password
records.description: Client key password for communication with Etcd.

records.name: alluxio.etcd.tls.enabled
records.description: Enable TLS for communication with Etcd.

records.name: alluxio.etcd.tls.client.no.endpoint.identification
records.description: The client has no endpoint identification

records.name: alluxio.etcd.keepalive.time
records.description: gRPC HTTP/2 keepalive ping interval for the jetcd client. When the channel is idle for this long, a PING is sent so a stalled etcd endpoint (e.g. during defrag) is detected and the client can recover the gRPC channel. Must be >= the etcd server's --grpc-keepalive-min-time (default 5s on etcd) or the server will send GOAWAY frames. To preserve an in-flight lease across a stall, this value plus alluxio.etcd.keepalive.timeout should be less than the lease TTL (alluxio.worker.failure.detection.timeout for workers, default 15s); otherwise this setting only bounds recovery time, it does not save the lease. With defaults (10s+3s=13s detection, 15s lease) the margin is a tight 2s — lower this to 5s for a 7s margin if your network can tolerate the extra keepalive PINGs.

records.name: alluxio.etcd.keepalive.timeout
records.description: How long the jetcd client waits for a keepalive PONG before marking the gRPC channel as broken. Lower values detect stalled endpoints faster but increase false-positive disconnects on lossy networks. Total detection latency is alluxio.etcd.keepalive.time + this value.

records.name: alluxio.etcd.keepalive.without.calls
records.description: Whether the jetcd client sends keepalive pings even when no RPCs are in flight. Required for failover when the only active stream (e.g. LeaseKeepAlive) is itself stalled on a hung etcd endpoint.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable the write buffer feature. When enabled, writes will go through a temporary buffer on workers before being persisted to the underlying storage.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.dual.buffer.file.system.type
records.description: The type of the dual write buffer file system.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.local.file.system.path
records.description: The local file system path for write buffer.
The regex to match files that will be written with rocksdb quorum write
The regex to match files that will be written with to local only

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.file.metadata.storage
records.description: The storage type for write buffer file metadata. Options are `MEMORY` for in-memory storage (for testing) and `FDB` for FoundationDB.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.async.check.orphan.timeout
records.description: This configuration specifies the timeout duration for orphan file detection.

records.name: alluxio.write.buffer.async.persist.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable asynchronous persistence of files from the write buffer to the underlying storage. If false, files are persisted upon close.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.async.persist.thread.pool.size
records.description: The size of the thread pool for asynchronously persisting files from the write buffer to the underlying storage.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.async.persist.submit.batch.size
records.description: The batch size of submitting async persist tasks.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.async.persist.schedule.period
records.description: The scheduling period for the asynchronous persistence service to check for and persist files from the write buffer.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.async.persist.cache.free.delay
records.description: The delay time for releasing the cache after data persistence is completed. A value less than or equal to 0 disables delayed release.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.async.file.check.period
records.description: The period for checking files in the write buffer. Files that have been in the buffer longer than this period will be prioritized for persistence.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.writer.thread.pool.size
records.description: The size of the thread pool used for writing data to multiple replicas in the write buffer in parallel.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.transient.copy.read.fallback.enabled
records.description: Whether to serve reads from the write cache when the requested path matches a configured transient-copy rule and the local copy is provably equivalent to the UFS view (matching length, mtime not newer than UFS). Disabling forces all such reads to go through the read buffer / UFS path.

records.name: alluxio.write.buffer.multipart.upload.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable multipart upload for the write buffer file system. When enabled, large writes are buffered in memory and flushed as part files, then merged at the metadata level on close.

records.name: alluxio.write.buffer.multipart.upload.part.size
records.description: The size of each part in bytes for write buffer multipart upload.

records.name: alluxio.write.buffer.multipart.upload.buffer.number
records.description: The number of buffers in the pool for write buffer multipart upload. Set to 0 to disable pooling: a new buffer is allocated on demand and dropped on release instead of being reused.

records.name: alluxio.write.buffer.multipart.upload.threads
records.description: The number of threads used for uploading parts in write buffer multipart upload.

records.name: alluxio.write.buffer.multipart.upload.max.buffers.per.stream
records.description: The maximum number of buffers a single multipart upload stream can hold concurrently. If the stream has reached this limit, further buffer allocation will block until an in-flight part write completes and returns its buffer. Set to 0 for unlimited (no per-stream limit).

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.async.persist.retry.initial.interval
records.description: The initial retry interval for async persist, and each subsequent retry interval will be twice as long as the previous one, until it reaches the maximum retry interval.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.async.persist.retry.max.interval
records.description: The maximum retry interval for async persist.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.write.cache.background.tasks.enabled
records.description: Whether enabled write cache background tasks.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.write.cache.background.tasks.worker.rpc.threads
records.description: The number of threads used for write cache background tasks worker RPCs.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scanner.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable the background write-cache TTL scanner that automatically purges expired files from the TRANSIENT write cache.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.refresh.enabled
records.description: Whether to allow the on-demand `ttl refresh` command, which walks a path prefix and retroactively stamps the write-cache TTL onto already-cached files. Disabled by default; enable deliberately, as a refresh can walk a large subtree.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scan.interval
records.description: How often the write-cache TTL scanner runs. Defaults to 1h to match the read-cache TTL eviction cadence (alluxio.ttl.eviction.check.interval); lower it for more prompt write-cache reclaim at higher coordinator/FDB scan cost, but not below unless that floor is also lowered.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scan.interval.min
records.description: Floor on the effective write-cache TTL scan interval; defaults to 1h to match the scan-interval default, so by default the scanner runs no more often than hourly. A configured below this floor is clamped up to it (with a warning), which also prevents an accidental tiny interval from tight-spinning the scanner. To scan more frequently, lower this floor too — do so cautiously: scanning faster than the drain can reclaim only adds coordinator/FDB load with no benefit.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scan.max.per.round
records.description: Maximum number of expired files the write-cache TTL scanner processes per scheduled run.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scan.batch.size
records.description: Number of TTL index entries fetched per FDB range-scan page inside the write-cache TTL scanner.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scan.threads
records.description: Number of threads the write-cache TTL scanner uses to purge expired files concurrently within a round. Higher values drain a backlog faster; each thread runs independent per-inode FDB transactions, made safe by the unlink CAS. Set to 1 for the legacy serial scan.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.write.cache.trash.gc.ttl
records.description: The minimum time a soft-deleted inode must remain in trash before being garbage collected. This grace period allows page store file cleanup to read write logs before they are removed.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.write.cache.async.persist.scan.inode.period
records.description: The period for scanning inodes for async persist.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.write.cache.async.persist.scan.inode.threads
records.description: The number of threads used for scanning inodes for async persist.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.write.cache.async.replica.check.scan.inode.period
records.description: The period for scanning inodes for async replica check.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.write.cache.async.replica.check.scan.inode.threads
records.description: The number of threads used for scanning inodes for async replica check.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.write.cache.async.replica.check.worker.offline.grace.period
records.description: The grace period before a lost worker triggers replica recovery. Workers that have been offline for less than this duration are still considered online for replica check purposes.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.write.cache.clean.invalid.locks.period
records.description: The period for cleaning invalid locks in write cache.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.write.cache.cleanup.invalid.locks.grace.duration
records.description: The grace duration before cleaning up invalid locks in write cache.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.write.cache.cleanup.temp.promote.file.period
records.description: The period for cleaning up temporary promote files.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.write.cache.cleanup.temp.promote.file.grace.duration
records.description: The grace duration before cleaning up temporary promote files.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.write.cache.cleanup.ufs.orphan.file.grace.duration
records.description: The grace duration before cleaning up UFS orphan files.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.write.cache.check.ufs.orphan.file.period
records.description: The period for checking UFS orphan files.

records.name: alluxio.coordinator.write.cache.check.ufs.orphan.file.threads
records.description: The number of threads used for checking UFS orphan files.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.orphan.file.grace.duration
records.description: The grace duration before cleaning up orphan files.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.page.store.unpinned.file.cleanup.period
records.description: How often the worker scans the page store for unpinned write-cache files to clean up. A value less than or equal to 0 disables the cleanup. Has no effect unless the write cache is enabled.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.page.store.unpinned.file.cleanup.grace.duration
records.description: An unpinned write-cache file is deleted from the page store only after the cleanup task has observed it unpinned for at least this duration, giving any in-flight reader a safe window before its pages are removed.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.page.store.unpinned.file.cleanup.max.pending
records.description: The maximum number of unpinned write-cache file ids held in the cleanup quarantine queue. When the queue is full the current scan stops and resumes after the next drain frees space (no file is lost, only deferred). Each queued id costs roughly 100 bytes, so the default of 1,000,000 caps the queue at about 100MB.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.async.copy.repersist.after.rename
records.description: Whether to re-persist the file after rename in transient copy task.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.async.copy.update.read.cache
records.description: Whether to clear the read cache for the UFS path after transient copy.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.async.copy.write.read.cache
records.description: Whether to write the file into read cache after transient copy.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.async.persist.warm.cache.enabled
records.description: Whether to warm data into read cache after async persist completes.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.async.persist.max.pending.tasks
records.description: The maximum number of pending tasks for async persist.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.async.persist.initial.retry.duration
records.description: The initial retry duration for async persist.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.async.persist.max.retry.duration
records.description: The maximum retry duration for async persist.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.async.persist.threads
records.description: The number of threads used for async persist.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.async.persist.queue.size
records.description: The queue size for async persist.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.check.orphan.file.threads
records.description: The number of threads used for checking orphan files.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.check.orphan.file.period
records.description: The period for checking orphan files.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.copy.missing.replica.threads
records.description: The number of threads used for copying missing replicas.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.copy.missing.replica.queue.size
records.description: The bounded queue size of the worker thread pool that copies missing write-cache replicas. When the queue is full new copy tasks are rejected and retried on the next replica-check round.

records.name: alluxio.worker.write.cache.compaction.threads
records.description: The number of threads used for compaction in write cache.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.trigger.async.persist.on.file.closed.enabled
records.description: Whether to trigger async persist when a file is closed.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.trigger.async.persist.on.file.flushed.enabled
records.description: Whether to trigger async persist when a file is flushed.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.trigger.async.persist.grace.duration.on.file.flushed
records.description: The grace duration for triggering async persist when a file is flushed.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.trigger.compaction.on.write.log.count
records.description: The number of write logs to trigger compaction.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.max.write.logs.per.block.for.compaction
records.description: The maximum number of write logs per block for compaction.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.compaction.space.amplification.percent
records.description: The space amplification percentage threshold that triggers write cache compaction. This value represents the allowed physical storage overhead relative to the logical file size as a percentage. For example, the default value of 50 means compaction is triggered when the physical file size reaches 150% of its logical size (i.e., the extra space occupied is 50% of the logical size). A value of 100 would mean compaction triggers when physical size is 200% of the logical size.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.compaction.space.amplification.min.file.size
records.description: The minimum logical file size required to trigger the space amplification check for write cache compaction. If a file's logical size is smaller than this threshold, compaction will not be triggered regardless of its space amplification ratio. This prevents unnecessary I/O overhead from compacting small files where the reclaimed space would be negligible.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.submit.compaction.task.to.worker.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable submitting compaction task to worker.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.metastore.cache.enabled
records.description: Whether to wrap the singleton FDB metastore instance with the local read-through cache.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.metastore.cache.positive.ttl
records.description: The TTL for positive entries in the write-cache metastore read-through cache.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.metastore.cache.negative.ttl
records.description: The TTL for negative entries in the write-cache metastore read-through cache. Set to 0sec to disable negative-entry caching.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.max.write.operation.number
records.description: The maximum number of write operations in random access stream. write cache flush will be triggered if the write operations number is more than the value we set.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.asycn.page.store.file.persist.task.thread.number
records.description: The thread number of write cache async page store file persist task.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.async.prefetch.enabled
records.description: If enabled, random access write cache streams prefetch data by asynchronous read-ahead operations with memory limits.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.async.prefetch.memory.limit
records.description: The memory limit for asynchronous read-ahead operations in random access write cache streams.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.async.prefetch.thread.number
records.description: The number of threads for asynchronous read-ahead operations in random access write cache streams.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.random.access.ufs.page.store.enabled
records.description: If enabled, random access write cache streams persist page-store data to a configured mounted UFS path instead of cache workers.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.random.access.ufs.page.store.path
records.description: The mounted Alluxio path used by random access write cache streams to persist page-store files when UFS page-store mode is enabled.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.multi.workspace.ufs.base.path
records.description: The base UFS path for storing write cache data when multi-workspace mode is enabled. Each workspace will create its own subdirectory under this path.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.in.memory.write.buffer.size
records.description: The maximum size of the in-memory write buffer for accumulating writes before flushing to the page store. Also used to determine compaction trigger thresholds.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.workspace.gc.time
records.description: The time after which an inactive workspace is eligible for garbage collection. Workspaces that have not been accessed within this duration may be cleaned up during periodic GC cycles.

records.name: alluxio.user.write.cache.workspace.orphan.file.check.period
records.description: The interval between periodic checks for orphan UFS files in the write cache workspace. Orphan files that are no longer referenced by any inode will be cleaned up.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.compaction.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable write log compaction for the write cache. When enabled, write logs within a block will be periodically merged to reduce the number of log entries and reclaim storage space.

records.name: alluxio.worker.mark.writing.files.duration
records.description: The duration a worker keeps the 'writing' mark for a file. The timer resets on every write. If no write occurs within this duration, the mark is removed.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.id.allocator.batch.size
records.description: The number of IDs to allocate per FDB transaction in the inode and write log ID allocators. A larger batch reduces FDB transactions but increases the gap in ID sequences when a node restarts.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.worker.id.cache.size
records.description: The maximum number of entries cached in each direction of the worker-id to long-reference mapping (see WorkerIdMapper). Bounds memory while avoiding FDB point lookups for recently seen worker references.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.block.size
records.description: The block size used by the write cache. Write logs and page store files are organized and compacted at block-level granularity.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.cluster.name
records.description: The name of the PFS cluster. Used to namespace FDB subspaces so that multiple clusters can share the same FoundationDB instance.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.scatter.buckets
records.description: The number of scatter buckets used by the inode ID allocator. This value is persisted in the cluster info table on first initialization and subsequent restarts will use the persisted value.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.cache.refresh.interval
records.description: How often the in-memory directory-quota cache is reloaded from FoundationDB. The cache is consulted on every write hot path instead of issuing per-write quota reads, so a shorter interval yields tighter enforcement at the cost of more background scans. setDirectoryQuota and clearDirectoryQuota refresh the cache synchronously in the calling process; other processes (e.g. the coordinator when a mutation is issued from a CLI JVM) catch up on their next scheduled tick — i.e. within this interval.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.counter.anchor.bits
records.description: Log2 of the number of anchor positions each directory-quota usage counter is scattered across in the FoundationDB inode keyspace (2^value anchors; default 8 = 256). More anchors spread a hot quota's counter writes over more storage teams, but cost proportionally more reads on cache refresh and getDirectoryQuota, so match it to the cluster's storage-team count. Expressed as a power of two so it can only change by integer multiples across versions, keeping the anchor set nested (a rolling restart stays correct once complete); values below 8 are raised to 8.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.backfill.scan.batch
records.description: How many child inodes a directory-quota backfill worker stamps (or strips) per FoundationDB transaction. The ceiling is driven by transaction-conflict waste, not FDB's 5s/10MB limits: a larger batch redoes more work when it collides with online writes, nested backfills, or async deletion on the same inodes. Lower it for deeply nested quotas or when backfill overlaps large recursive deletes.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.backfill.worker.threads
records.description: Size of the directory-quota backfill worker thread pool. Bounds the number of concurrent backfill write transactions against FoundationDB.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.backfill.scan.interval
records.description: How often the directory-quota backfill leader polls the backfill/unbackfill queues for items to dispatch and for COMPUTING/CLEARING quotas whose queues have drained.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.backfill.batch.delay
records.description: Test/debug throttle: an artificial delay a directory-quota backfill worker sleeps after each processed batch, slowing the backfill (regardless of subtree shape) so the COMPUTING/CLEARING window stays open long enough to observe or exercise concurrent operations. Default 0 disables it; leave it 0 in production.

records.name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.orphan.cleanup.interval
records.description: How often the directory-quota backfill leader sweeps leaked usage counters: counter slots whose quota id no longer has a limit row (left behind when a non-empty quota-bearing directory is deleted and its descendants' delete-time decrements recreate slots after the inline cleanup removed the row). The leak is benign for enforcement (a quota with no limit row is never loaded into the enforce cache), so this is a low-frequency, best-effort sweep that runs only when the leader's queues are idle.

records.name: alluxio.jvm.monitor.minor.pause.frequency.threshold
records.description: When the ratio of the total time of minor pauses in a fixed time window exceeds this threshold, flag as if a severe pause has happened.

records.name: alluxio.jvm.monitor.warn.threshold
records.description: When the JVM pauses for anything longer than this, log a WARN message.

records.name: alluxio.jvm.monitor.minor.pause.threshold
records.description: When the JVM pauses for anything longer than this, log an INFO message.

records.name: alluxio.jvm.monitor.sleep.interval
records.description: The time for the JVM monitor thread to sleep.

records.name: alluxio.master.jvm.monitor.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable start JVM monitor thread on the master. This will start a thread to detect JVM-wide pauses induced by GC or other reasons.

records.name: alluxio.worker.jvm.monitor.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable start JVM monitor thread on the worker. This will start a thread to detect JVM-wide pauses induced by GC or other reasons.

records.name: alluxio.standalone.fuse.jvm.monitor.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable start JVM monitor thread on the standalone fuse process. This will start a thread to detect JVM-wide pauses induced by GC or other reasons.

records.name: alluxio.hadoop.security.authentication
records.description: HDFS authentication method.

records.name: alluxio.hadoop.security.krb5.conf
records.description: Kerberos krb file for configuration of Kerberos.

records.name: alluxio.hadoop.kerberos.keytab.login.autorenewal
records.description: Kerberos authentication keytab login auto renew.

records.name: alluxio.user.dynamic.consistent.hash.ring.enabled
records.description: If true, use live worker list to build consistent hash ring. Otherwise, use all worker list that includes lost workers to build consistent hash ring.

records.name: alluxio.worker.http.server.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable worker HTTP server.

records.name: alluxio.worker.http.server.port
records.description: The port Alluxio's worker's HTTP server runs on.

records.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable the OCI Distribution V2 registry mirror (/v2/...) on the worker HTTP server. When enabled, the worker fronts an upstream OCI registry such as Azure ACR. See design/Azure-ACR/alluxio_acr_v02_refined_full_design_cn.md.

records.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.upstream.allowlist
records.description: Comma-separated allowlist of upstream registry hostnames the OCI mirror is permitted to forward to (e.g. "myacr.azurecr.io,secondacr.azurecr.io"). Empty means "allow any host" -- useful for development but unsafe in production, since a misconfigured oci-proxy could otherwise direct the worker to fetch from arbitrary internet hosts.

records.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.persistent.cache.enabled
records.description: Whether the OCI registry handler decides cache hits by probing the worker page store directly (via CacheManager.getUsage) instead of consulting the in-memory mBlobMetaCache. When true, blob bytes that survive a worker restart can still be served from the on-disk cache, at the cost of one upstream HEAD request per cold (in-memory) lookup. Recommended for production once validated.

records.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.blacklist.etcd.path
records.description: Etcd parent path where the OCI mirror watches for cache-bypass entries. Each child key represents one URL-escaped `<registry>/<repo>` (exact-match) that should bypass both the in-memory manifest/blob meta caches and the worker page store. Used to opt out frequently-mutated images (e.g. tags like `latest`, `developer`, or large Windows base images) from cache logic so that tag rewrites take effect immediately. Workers update their view via an etcd watch.

records.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.authz.ttl
records.description: TTL of the repo-level AuthZ short cache (positive verdicts). Shorter values make permission revocations propagate faster at the cost of more upstream auth checks. Tokens that expire sooner than this value will still be honoured by the cache up to the configured TTL, so do not raise it above the typical upstream token lifetime.

records.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.authz.negative.ttl
records.description: TTL of negative (deny) AuthZ cache entries. Implemented via a scheduled invalidate so a deny verdict yields the cache sooner than a normal positive entry; lets a user that just received new permissions pull successfully without waiting for the full positive TTL.

records.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.binding.ttl
records.description: TTL of the repo-scoped object binding cache (digest ↔ repo safety fence) AND of the blob metadata cache. The two are intentionally linked: a metadata entry must never outlive its binding, otherwise the handler could serve cached bytes whose authorization fence has expired. Longer values reduce upstream HEAD traffic; shorter values force more frequent re-validation.

records.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.tag.soft.ttl
records.description: Soft TTL for tag → digest bindings. Below this age, cached bindings are served without contacting upstream. After this age but before the hard TTL, the binding is still served but the next request triggers an upstream HEAD revalidation so a tag that has been re-pushed is detected promptly. Must be ≤ the hard TTL.

records.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.tag.hard.ttl
records.description: Hard TTL for tag → digest bindings. After this age the entry is fully evicted and the next request takes the slow GET-manifest path.

records.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.blob.singleflight.timeout
records.description: Maximum time a blob singleflight follower waits for the leader's cold pull to populate the cache before falling through to its own direct upstream fetch. A follower fall-through costs one extra upstream GET; the old 10min value could stall a stampede of clients on a wedged leader. Raise it only for genuinely huge (multi-GB) layers over a slow upstream.

records.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.blob.stream.timeout
records.description: Upper bound a blob streamer thread waits for a single chunk write to the client channel to complete under backpressure. Caps how long a slow or vanished client can pin a streamer thread; pairs with the HTTP idle timeout.

records.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.blob.range.prefetch.threads
records.description: Max concurrent background full-blob backfills triggered by range misses. Saturation skips the backfill (the range was already served from upstream); raise for K8s cold-start bursts where many nodes range-miss the same large image at once.

records.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.blob.range.prefetch.queue
records.description: Queue capacity for the range-miss blob backfill pool (see ).

records.name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.upstream.throttle.cooldown
records.description: When the upstream registry returns a 429/503 rate-limit response, the worker records a short per-(registry, repo) cooldown and serves an immediate 429 (with Retry-After) to further requests for that repo instead of hammering the already-throttling upstream. The cooldown honors the upstream Retry-After header but is capped at this value. 0 disables the local cooldown (Retry-After is still propagated to clients).

records.name: alluxio.worker.http.server.connection.idle.timeout
records.description: Idle timeout for connections on the worker HTTP server (the :28080 REST / OCI-mirror pipeline). When a connection has neither read nor written for this long, it is closed, reclaiming file descriptors from clients that vanished without sending a TCP RST (container OOM, NAT drop). Active transfers keep the channel non-idle so they are not affected. 0 disables idle detection.

records.name: alluxio.oci.registry.credential.provider
records.description: Name of the OciCredentialProvider used to mint an upstream OCI registry pull credential when there is no client-supplied token (image preload). Selected by name from the SPI implementations registered under alluxio.oci.auth.OciCredentialProviderFactory. The default 'azure-workload-identity' performs the keyless Azure Workload Identity -> ACR token exchange. Used by both the Coordinator (layout scan) and the worker (blob fetch); the serving path is unaffected and keeps using the client's token.

records.name: alluxio.oci.registry.static.token
records.description: Pre-supplied upstream registry Bearer token used by the 'static-token' OCI credential provider. Defaults to the OCI_REGISTRY_STATIC_TOKEN environment variable. Intended for externally injected/short-lived tokens (e.g. an ACR access token) where the worker does not mint its own; the token is returned as-is for every repository.

records.name: alluxio.oci.registry.azure.client.id
records.description: Override for the Azure AD application (client) id used by the azure-workload-identity credential provider. Defaults to the AZURE_CLIENT_ID environment variable injected by the Workload Identity webhook; set this only to override that injected value.

records.name: alluxio.oci.registry.azure.tenant.id
records.description: Override for the Azure AD tenant id used by the azure-workload-identity credential provider. Defaults to the AZURE_TENANT_ID environment variable injected by the Workload Identity webhook.

records.name: alluxio.oci.registry.azure.federated.token.file
records.description: Override for the path to the kubelet-projected ServiceAccount token used as the client_assertion by the azure-workload-identity credential provider. Defaults to the AZURE_FEDERATED_TOKEN_FILE environment variable injected by the Workload Identity webhook.

records.name: alluxio.oci.registry.azure.authority.host
records.description: Azure AD authority host the azure-workload-identity credential provider posts the client_credentials grant to. Defaults to the public cloud; override (or set AZURE_AUTHORITY_HOST) for sovereign clouds.

records.name: alluxio.oci.registry.azure.acr.scope
records.description: AAD scope identifying the ACR data plane, requested when the azure-workload-identity credential provider exchanges the projected token for an AAD access token. Must point at ACR; the ARM scope is rejected by ACR's /oauth2/exchange endpoint.

records.name: alluxio.user.network.netty.channel.pool.size.max
records.description: The maximum number of netty channels cached in the netty channel pool.

records.name: alluxio.user.network.netty.channel.pool.gc.threshold
records.description: A netty channel is closed if it has been idle for more than this threshold.

records.name: alluxio.user.network.netty.writer.packet.size.bytes
records.description: When a client writes to a remote worker, the maximum packet size.

records.name: alluxio.user.network.netty.writer.buffer.size.packets
records.description: When a client writes to a remote worker, the maximum number of packets to buffer by the client.

records.name: alluxio.user.network.netty.reader.buffer.size.packets
records.description: When a client reads from a remote worker, the maximum number of packets to buffer by the client.

records.name: alluxio.user.network.netty.channel.pool.disabled
records.description: Disable netty channel pool. This should be turned on if the client version is >= 1.3.0 but server version is <= 1.2.x.

records.name: alluxio.user.network.netty.writer.close.timeout
records.description: The timeout to close a netty writer client.

records.name: alluxio.user.network.netty.timeout
records.description: The maximum time for a netty client (for block reads and block writes) to wait for a response from the data server.

records.name: alluxio.dora.client.ufs.fallback.enabled
records.description: Whether the client automatically falls back to using the UFS for I/O operations when Alluxio service becomes unavailable.

records.name: alluxio.dora.client.metadata.cache.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable metadata cache for dora client. This is only valid for read only workloads.

records.name: alluxio.dora.worker.metastore.rocksdb.dir
records.description: The base dir of RocksDB to store Dora metadata

records.name: alluxio.dora.worker.metastore.rocksdb.ttl
records.default: "-1s") // -1s means no expiry .setDescription("The TTL (Time To Live) in duration of RocksDB of Dora metadata. " + "0s or negative value means no expiry"
records.description: The TTL (Time To Live) in duration of RocksDB of Dora metadata. 0s or negative value means no expiry

records.name: alluxio.dora.load.job.must.check.quota
records.description: The load job is associated with quota. If it is associated,the load job needs the quota information to meet the load requirements.

records.name: alluxio.dora.job.load.quota.mutual.exclusive
records.description: Need to limit the number of LoadJobs running at most to one under the same quota?

records.name: alluxio.dora.worker.metastore.rocksdb.bloom.filter
records.description: Whether or not to use a bloom filter in the Block meta table in RocksDB. If unset, the RocksDB default will be used. See https://github.com/facebook/rocksdb/wiki/RocksDB-Bloom-Filter

records.name: alluxio.dora.worker.metastore.rocksdb.cache.size
records.description: The capacity in bytes of the RocksDB block metadata table LRU cache. If unset, the RocksDB default will be used. See https://github.com/facebook/rocksdb/wiki/Block-Cache. Our microbench test shows that 1GB cache can double the metadata read operation performance for a meta store that persists 10M metadata entries. Note that the memory is off-heap and does not count in jvm memory.

records.name: alluxio.dora.worker.metastore.rocksdb.block.index
records.description: The block index type to be used in the RocksDB block metadata table. If unset, the RocksDB default will be used.See https://rocksdb.org/blog/2018/08/23/data-block-hash-index.html

records.name: alluxio.dora.worker.metastore.rocksdb.index
records.description: The index type to be used in the RocksDB block metadata table. If unset, the RocksDB default will be used. See https://github.com/facebook/rocksdb/wiki/Index-Block-Format

records.name: alluxio.dora.worker.metastore.rocksdb.cleanup.enabled
records.description: When set to true, the system will periodically cleanup expired metadata entries in the RocksDB-based metastore on workers, helping to optimize storage usage and maintain performance.

records.name: alluxio.dora.worker.metastore.rocksdb.cleanup.interval
records.description: The interval between automatic cleanup runs for expired metadata in RocksDB metastore

records.name: alluxio.dora.worker.metastore.rocksdb.cleanup.batch.size
records.description: The batch size for cleaning up expired metadata in RocksDB metastore. When expired entries are found, they will be deleted in batches of this size.

records.name: alluxio.dora.worker.page.meta.compaction.interval
records.description: The delay between PageMetaCF tombstone-compaction rounds. Each round compacts at most a few of the densest SST files, so a short interval keeps tombstone pressure low while a single round stays cheap.

records.name: alluxio.dora.worker.page.meta.compaction.tombstone.ratio
records.description: An SST file in PageMetaCF is eligible for tombstone compaction only when the fraction of its entries that are delete tombstones reaches this ratio. Lower values compact more aggressively at the cost of more write amplification.

records.name: alluxio.dora.worker.page.meta.compaction.min.tombstones
records.description: An SST file in PageMetaCF is eligible for tombstone compaction only when it holds at least this many delete tombstones. Guards against compacting small files whose high tombstone ratio represents little reclaimable space.

records.name: alluxio.dora.worker.page.meta.store.rocksdb.enabled
records.description: When set to true, the worker persists page metadata in the RocksDB-backed metastore so it survives worker restarts. When false (default), page metadata is kept in an in-memory store.

records.name: alluxio.client.write.to.ufs.enabled
records.description: Whether or not to enable client writing data directly to UFS. If enabled, client writes data to worker (to be cached in Paging Store) and to UFS (to be stored permanently). Client need UFS credentials in this case. Enabled by default.

records.name: alluxio.client.list.status.from.ufs.enabled
records.description: Whether or not to enable client list status directly from UFS,with the UFS iterateStatus API, bypassing worker

records.name: alluxio.client.index.service.list.batch.size
records.description: Batch size for listing directories. Default to 1000 which aligns with most object storage systems.

records.name: alluxio.client.index.service.parallelism
records.description: Number of threads used by the client index service

records.name: alluxio.client.index.service.list.timeout
records.description: Absolute deadline applied to the server-streaming listChildren RPC. Set generously because legitimate large-directory listings (e.g. prewarm of a million-file bucket) take real time; a stuck worker freezes the caller for up to this duration, but pre-fix behavior was an unbounded hang (AC-7179). Unary index service RPCs (addChild, removeChild, invalidate) use the generic alluxio.user.rpc.retry.max.duration timeout instead so they fail fast. Acts as a backstop alongside the per-batch inactivity timeout (alluxio.client.index.service.list.inactivity.timeout) — whichever fires first cancels the stream.

records.name: alluxio.client.index.service.list.inactivity.timeout
records.description: Per-batch inactivity timeout for the server-streaming listChildren RPC. When > 0, cancels the stream if the server fails to deliver the next batch within this window. Because batches arriving from the server reset the timer, this lets legitimate long listings run indefinitely while still failing fast on a worker that is stuck or blackholed mid-stream — the freeze the user sees is bounded by this value, not by the absolute listChildren deadline. Default 0 disables the inactivity timer entirely; the absolute listChildren deadline (alluxio.client.index.service.list.timeout) is then the only bound. The default is conservative pending the worker-side cancellation handling tracked under AC-7212 — set to a positive value (e.g. 30sec) to opt in once the worker side is ready.

records.name: alluxio.client.index.service.client.cache.idle.timeout
records.description: Idle timeout for cached index service clients. Entries that have not been accessed for this duration are evicted and their underlying gRPC channel is closed. Bounds long-tail resource accumulation when worker pods churn (every dead pod that the cache talked to would otherwise keep its channel alive forever). Default 1day favors avoiding spurious reconnects on sparse FUSE workloads at the cost of some log noise from dead-channel reconnect attempts (~720/day per evicted pod). Lower (e.g. 1h) for high-churn environments where many pods are recycled per day.

records.name: alluxio.user.fuse.hard.link.return.exdev.enabled
records.description: Enables a workaround for RocksDB versions older than 7.1.1 that do not handle EOPNOTSUPP correctly. When enabled, Alluxio FUSE will return EXDEV instead of EOPNOTSUPP for hard link operations. This is primarily needed for compatibility with older RocksDB versions (e.g., dependencies in integration tests). Newer RocksDB versions do not require this setting.

records.name: alluxio.user.fuse.cross.quota.domain.return.exdev.enabled
records.description: Controls the errno FUSE returns when a rename or hard link is rejected because the source and destination are in different write-cache directory-quota domains. When false (default), it returns EOPNOTSUPP and the operation fails outright without copying. When true, it returns EXDEV, so coreutils mv transparently falls back to copy+delete (the copy is re-created and counted in the destination domain).

records.description: Whether SSL connection is enabled in OPA daemon

records.name: alluxio.security.authorization.plugin.opa.ssl.ca.path
records.description: If SSL is enabled, ca file path is expected.

records.description: If SSL is enabled, cert file path is expected.

records.name: alluxio.security.authorization.plugin.opa.ssl.key.path
records.description: If SSL is enabled, key file path is expected.

records.name: alluxio.security.authorization.plugin.opa.policy.path
records.description: The policy path and name

records.description: The time in seconds to cache one OPA decision.

records.description: The cache capacity in number of record.

records.description: Check ACL at file level otherwise directory level

records.description: Retry times in network error

records.name: alluxio.security.authorization.cache.expiration.time
records.description: The expiration time for each entry in the authorization cache.

records.name: alluxio.security.authorization.cache.max.size
records.description: The maximum number of entries in the authorization cache.

records.name: alluxio.security.secret.store.enabled
records.description: Enable Secret Store.

records.name: alluxio.security.secret.store.renew.interval
records.description: Periodical auto-renew check time for secrets and auth credential, -1 means periodical auto-renew is not enabled.

records.name: alluxio.security.secret.store.hashicorp.vault.enabled
records.description: Enable the Vault secret provider for Secret Store.

records.name: alluxio.security.secret.store.hashicorp.vault.authentication
records.description: The authentication method of secret store. Current support auth method: TOKEN.

records.name: alluxio.security.secret.store.hashicorp.vault.address
records.description: Sets the address (URL) of the Vault server instance to which API calls should be sent.

records.name: alluxio.security.secret.store.hashicorp.vault.token
records.description: The token used to authenticate with Vault.

records.name: alluxio.security.secret.store.hashicorp.vault.cache.enabled
records.description: Enable the cache of vault secrets.

records.name: alluxio.security.secret.store.hashicorp.vault.open.timeout
records.description: The time to wait before giving up on establishing an HTTP(S) connection to the Vault server.

records.name: alluxio.security.secret.store.hashicorp.vault.read.timeout
records.description: After an HTTP(S) connection has already been established, this is the time to wait for all data to finish downloading.

records.name: alluxio.security.secret.store.hashicorp.vault.client.retry
records.description: The number of times that vault client will be retried when a failure occurs.

records.name: alluxio.security.secret.store.hashicorp.vault.client.retry.interval
records.description: The number of ms that the vault client will wait in between retries.

records.name: alluxio.security.secret.store.hashicorp.vault.secret.renew.threshold
records.description: The renew threshold of auth credential, vault secret provider will renew secrets automatically if TTL of secret is shorter than threshold.

records.name: alluxio.security.secret.store.hashicorp.vault.auth.renew.threshold
records.description: The renew threshold of auth credential, vault secret provider will renew auth credential automatically if TTL of secret is shorter than threshold.

records.name: alluxio.security.secret.store.hashicorp.vault.secret.incremental.time
records.description: The incremental leasing time of vault secret.

records.name: alluxio.security.secret.store.hashicorp.vault.auth.incremental.time
records.description: The incremental leasing time of auth credential, -1 means use default incremental lease time in vault server.

records.name: alluxio.concurrent.write.enabled
records.description: Whether to enable concurrent writing. When this configuration is set to true, it will simultaneously change the behavior of both FUSE and the worker: FUSE will convert all write locks into read locks, and the worker’s handle will allow writing to multiple files concurrently.
